NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the tent of meeting, on the first day of the second month, in the second year after they had come out of the land of Egypt, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses in the desert of Sinai in the tent of meeting,—on the first of the second month in the second year by their coming forth out of the land of Egypt saying:

text.NRSV: Take a census of the whole congregation of Israelites, in their clans, by ancestral houses, according to the number of names, every male individually;
text.Rotherham: Reckon ye up the sum of all the assembly of the sons of Israel, by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, every male, by their polls;

text.NRSV: from twenty years old and upward, everyone in Israel able to go to war. You and Aaron shall enroll them, company by company.
text.Rotherham: from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war in Israel,—ye shall number them by their hosts, thou and Aaron;

text.NRSV: A man from each tribe shall be with you, each man the head of his ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: and with you, shall be one man for each tribe,—each man, head of his ancestral house, shall be.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the men who shall assist you: From Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur.
text.Rotherham: These, then, are the names of the men who shall stand with you,—Of Reuben, Elizur, son of Shedeur;

text.NRSV: From Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
text.Rotherham: Of Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;

text.NRSV: From Judah, Nahshon son of Amminadab.
text.Rotherham: Of Judah, Nahshon, son of Amminadab;

text.NRSV: From Issachar, Nethanel son of Zuar.
text.Rotherham: Of Issachar, Nethanel, son of Zuar;

text.NRSV: From Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon.
text.Rotherham: Of Zebulun, Eliab, son of Helon;

text.NRSV: From the sons of Joseph: from Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud; from Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Joseph,—Of Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud; Of Manasseh, Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur;

text.NRSV: From Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni.
text.Rotherham: Of Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni;

text.NRSV: From Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
text.Rotherham: Of Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;

text.NRSV: From Asher, Pagiel son of Ochran.
text.Rotherham: Of Asher, Pagiel, son of Ochran;

text.NRSV: From Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel.
text.Rotherham: Of Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel;

text.NRSV: From Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.
text.Rotherham: Of Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.

text.NRSV: These were the ones chosen from the congregation, the leaders of their ancestral tribes, the heads of the divisions of Israel.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who had been summoned by the assembly, being princes of the tribes of their fathers,—heads of the thousands of Israel.

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name,
text.Rotherham: So then Moses and Aaron took these men, who were distinguished by name;

text.NRSV: and on the first day of the second month they assembled the whole congregation together. They registered themselves in their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names from twenty years old and upward, individually,
text.Rotherham: all the assembly also, called they together on the first of the second month,—and they declared their pedigree according to their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names from twenty years old and upwards by their polls.

text.NRSV: as the Lord commanded Moses. He enrolled them in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.Rotherham: As Yahweh commanded Moses, so he mustered them in the desert of Sinai.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Reuben, Israel's firstborn, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, individually, every male from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: And they were [as followeth] —The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel, in their pedigrees—by their families, by their ancestral houses, in the counting of names, by their polls, every male, from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Reuben were forty-six thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Reuben, forty-six thousand and five hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Simeon, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, those of them that were numbered, according to the number of names, individually, every male from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Simeon, in their pedigrees by their families by their ancestral houses,—such as were numbered of him, in the counting of names by their polls, every male from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Simeon were fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Simeon, fifty-nine thousand and three hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Gad, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Gad, in their pedigrees—by their families by their ancestral houses, in the counting of names from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war:

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Gad were forty-five thousand six hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Gad, forty-five thousand and six hundred and fifty.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Judah, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Judah, in their pedigrees by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Judah were seventy-four thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Judah, seventy-four thousand and six hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Issachar, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Issachar, in their pedigrees—by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty year old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Issachar were fifty-four thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Issachar, fifty-four thousand and four hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Zebulun, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Zebulun, in their pedigrees—by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Zebulun were fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Zebulun, fifty-seven thousand and four hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Joseph, namely, the descendants of Ephraim, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Joseph—Of the sons of Ephraim, in their pedigrees—by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty years old, and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Ephraim were forty thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them, as belonging to the tribe of Ephraim, forty thousand and five hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Manasseh, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Manasseh, in their pedigrees—by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Manasseh were thirty-two thousand two hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Manasseh, thirty-two thousand, and two hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Benjamin, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Benjamin, in their pedigrees by their families, by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty years old, and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Benjamin were thirty-five thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Benjamin, thirty-five thousand and four hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Dan, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Dan, in their pedigrees by their families by their ancestral houses, in the counting of names from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Dan were sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Dan, sixty-two thousand, and seven hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Asher, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Asher, in their pedigrees—by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty years old, and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Asher were forty-one thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Asher, forty-one thousand, and five hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Naphtali, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: The sons of Naphtali, in their pedigrees by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty years old, and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Naphtali were fifty-three thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them, as belonging to the tribe of Naphtali, fifty-three thousand, and four hundred.

text.NRSV: These are those who were enrolled, whom Moses and Aaron enrolled with the help of the leaders of Israel, twelve men, each representing his ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who were numbered, whom Moses and Aaron and the twelve princes of Israel did number, the princes acting each one for his ancestral house.

text.NRSV: So the whole number of the Israelites, by their ancestral houses, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war in Israel—
text.Rotherham: So then all they who were numbered of the sons of Israel by their ancestral houses, from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war in Israel;

text.NRSV: their whole number was six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: so then all they who were numbered were six hundred and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.

text.NRSV: The Levites, however, were not numbered by their ancestral tribe along with them.
text.Rotherham: But the Levites by the tribe of their fathers were not numbered in their midst.

text.NRSV: The Lord had said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:

text.NRSV: Only the tribe of Levi you shall not enroll, and you shall not take a census of them with the other Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Only the tribe of Levi, shalt thou not number, nor the sum of them, shalt thou reckon up, in the midst of the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: Rather you shall appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the covenant, and over all its equipment, and over all that belongs to it; they are to carry the tabernacle and all its equipment, and they shall tend it, and shall camp around the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: But do, thou thyself, put the Levites in charge over the habitation of the testimony, and over all the furniture thereof and over all that pertaineth thereto, they, shall bear the habitation, and all the furniture thereof, and they, shall attend thereupon; and, round about the habitation, shall they encamp.

text.NRSV: When the tabernacle is to set out, the Levites shall take it down; and when the tabernacle is to be pitched, the Levites shall set it up. And any outsider who comes near shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, when the habitation is to set forward, the Levites shall take it down, and when the habitation is to be pitched, the Levites, shall set it up, but the stranger that draweth near, shall be put to death.

text.NRSV: The other Israelites shall camp in their respective regimental camps, by companies;
text.Rotherham: So then the sons of Israel shall pitch their tents, every man, near his own camp, and, every man, near his own standard, by their hosts;

text.NRSV: but the Levites shall camp around the tabernacle of the covenant, so that there may be no wrath on the congregation of the Israelites; and the Levites shall perform the guard duty of the tabernacle of the covenant.
text.Rotherham: but the Levites, shall pitch their tents round about the habitation of the testimony, that there be no wrath upon the assembly of the sons of Israel,—so shall the Levites keep the watch of the habitation of the testimony.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did so; they did just as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: Thus did the sons of Israel,—according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did they.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh onto Moses and onto Aaron saying:

text.NRSV: The Israelites shall camp each in their respective regiments, under signs by their ancestral houses; they shall camp facing the tent of meeting on every side.
text.Rotherham: Every man—near his standard with the ensigns belonging to their ancestral houses, shall the sons of Israel encamp,—at a distance round about the tent of meeting, shall they encamp.

text.NRSV: Those to camp on the east side toward the sunrise shall be of the standard of the camp of Judah by companies; the leader of the people of Judah being Nahshon son of Amminadab,
text.Rotherham: And they who encamp eastwards towards sunrise, shall be the standard of the camp of Judah by their hosts,—even the prince of the sons of Judah, Nashon, son of Amminadab;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of seventy-four thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—seventy-four thousand and six hundred,

text.NRSV: Those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Issachar, the leader of the Issacharites being Nethanel son of Zuar,
text.Rotherham: Then they who encamp by him the tribe of Issachar,—even the prince of the sons of Issachar, Nethanel, son of Zuar;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of fifty-four thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host even they who are numbered of them,—fifty-four thousand and four hundred.

text.NRSV: Then the tribe of Zebulun: the leader of the Zebulunites being Eliab son of Helon,
text.Rotherham: The tribe of Zebulun, even the prince of the sons of Zebulun, Eliab, son of Helon;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of him, fifty-seven thousand and four hundred.

text.NRSV: The total enrollment of the camp of Judah, by companies, is one hundred eighty-six thousand four hundred. They shall set out first on the march.
text.Rotherham: All they who are num-bered to the camp of Judah. a hundred and eighty-six thousand four hundred, by their hosts, shall first set forward.

text.NRSV: On the south side shall be the standard of the camp of Reuben by companies; the leader of the Reubenites being Elizur son of Shedeur,
text.Rotherham: The standard of the camp of Reuben southwards, by their hosts,—even the prince of the sons of Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of forty-six thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of him,—forty-six thousand, and five hundred.

text.NRSV: And those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Simeon, the leader of the Simeonites being Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai,
text.Rotherham: Then they who encamp by him, the tribe of Simeon,—even the prince of the sons of Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—fifty-nine thousand and three hundred.

text.NRSV: Then the tribe of Gad: the leader of the Gadites being Eliasaph son of Reuel,
text.Rotherham: Then the tribe of Gad, even the prince of the sons of Gad, Eliasaph son of Reuel;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of forty-five thousand six hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—forty-five thousand, and six hundred and fifty.

text.NRSV: The total enrollment of the camp of Reuben, by companies, is one hundred fifty-one thousand four hundred fifty. They shall set out second.
text.Rotherham: All they who are num-bered to the camp of Reuben,—a hundred and fifty-one thousand and four hundred and fifty, by their hosts, and they in the second rank, shall set forward.

text.NRSV: The tent of meeting, with the camp of the Levites, shall set out in the center of the camps; they shall set out just as they camp, each in position, by their regiments.
text.Rotherham: Then shall set forward the tent of meeting—the camp of the Levites in the midst of the camps,—as they encamp, so, shall they set forward, every man at the side thereof by their standards.

text.NRSV: On the west side shall be the standard of the camp of Ephraim by companies; the leader of the people of Ephraim being Elishama son of Ammihud,
text.Rotherham: the standard of the camp of Ephraim, by their hosts westwards,—even the prince of the sons of Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of forty thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—forty thousand, and five hundred.

text.NRSV: Next to him shall be the tribe of Manasseh, the leader of the people of Manasseh being Gamaliel son of Pedahzur,
text.Rotherham: Then by him, the tribe of Manasseh, even the prince of the sons of Manasseh, Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of thirty-two thousand two hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—thirty-two thousand and two hundred.

text.NRSV: Then the tribe of Benjamin: the leader of the Benjaminites being Abidan son of Gideoni,
text.Rotherham: Then the tribe of Benjamin, even the prince of the sons of Benjamin, Abidan, son of Gideoni;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of thirty-five thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—thirty-five thousand and four hundred.

text.NRSV: The total enrollment of the camp of Ephraim, by companies, is one hundred eight thousand one hundred. They shall set out third on the march.
text.Rotherham: All they who are numbered to the camp of Ephraim a hundred and eight thousand and one hundred by their hosts, and they in the third rank, shall set forward.

text.NRSV: On the north side shall be the standard of the camp of Dan by companies; the leader of the Danites being Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai,
text.Rotherham: the standard of the camp of Dan, north-wards by their hosts, even the prince of the sons of Dan, Ahiezer, son of Ammishaddai:

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—sixty-two thousand and seven hundred.

text.NRSV: Those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Asher, the leader of the Asherites being Pagiel son of Ochran,
text.Rotherham: Then they who encamp by him the tribe of Asher,—even the prince of the sons of Asher, Pagiel, son of Ochran;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of forty-one thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—forty-one thousand and five hundred.

text.NRSV: Then the tribe of Naphtali: the leader of the Naphtalites being Ahira son of Enan,
text.Rotherham: Then the tribe of Naphtali,—even the prince of the sons of Naphtali, Ahira, son of Enan;

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of fifty-three thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—fifty-three thousand and four hundred,

text.NRSV: The total enrollment of the camp of Dan is one hundred fifty-seven thousand six hundred. They shall set out last, by companies.
text.Rotherham: All they who are numbered to the camp of Dan, a hundred and fifty-seven thousand and six hundred, in the hindmost rank, shall they set forward, by their standards.

text.NRSV: This was the enrollment of the Israelites by their ancestral houses; the total enrollment in the camps by their companies was six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who were numbered of the sons of Israel by their ancestral houses,—all they who were numbered of the camps by their hosts were six hundred and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.

text.NRSV: But the Levites were not enrolled among the other Israelites, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: But the Levites, were not numbered in the midst of the sons of Israel,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did just so; they encamped by their standards, and so they set out, everyone in his clan, according to his ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: Thus did the sons of Israel,—according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did they encamp by their standards, and so, did they set forward, every one by his families near his ancestral house.

text.NRSV: This is the lineage of Aaron and Moses at the time when the Lord spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
text.Rotherham: Now, these, were the pedigrees of Aaron and Moses,—in the day when Yahweh spake with Moses in Mount Sinai.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar;
text.Rotherham: And these, are the names of the sons of Aaron—the firstborn Nadab,—and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

text.NRSV: these are the names of the sons of Aaron, the anointed priests, whom he ordained to minister as priests.
text.Rotherham: These, are the names of the sons of Aaron, the priests that were anointed,—who were installed to minister as priests.

text.NRSV: But Nadab and Abihu died before the Lord when they offered unholy fire before the Lord in the wilderness of Sinai, and they had no children. So Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests in the lifetime of their father Aaron.
text.Rotherham: But Nadab and Abihu died before Yahweh when they brought near strange fire before Yahweh in the desert of Sinai, and sons, had they none,—so then Eleazar and Ithamar ministered as priests, in the presence of Aaron their father.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—

text.NRSV: Bring the tribe of Levi near, and set them before Aaron the priest, so that they may assist him.
text.Rotherham: Bring near the tribe of Levi, and thou shalt cause it to stand before Aaron the priest,—so shall they wait upon him;

text.NRSV: They shall perform duties for him and for the whole congregation in front of the tent of meeting, doing service at the tabernacle;
text.Rotherham: so shall they keep his charge, and the charge of all the assembly, before the tent of meeting,—to do the laborious work of the habitation;

text.NRSV: they shall be in charge of all the furnishings of the tent of meeting, and attend to the duties for the Israelites as they do service at the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: so shall they have charge of all the furniture of the tent of meeting, even the charge of the sons of Israel,—to do the laborious work of the habitation.

text.NRSV: You shall give the Levites to Aaron and his descendants; they are unreservedly given to him from among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Thus shalt thou give the Levites unto Aaron and unto his sons,—given, given, they are unto him, from among the sons of Israel;

text.NRSV: But you shall make a register of Aaron and his descendants; it is they who shall attend to the priesthood, and any outsider who comes near shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: but unto Aaron, and unto his sons, shalt thou give oversight, so shall they keep charge of their priesthood,—and, the stranger who cometh near, shall be put to death.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—

text.NRSV: I hereby accept the Levites from among the Israelites as substitutes for all the firstborn that open the womb among the Israelites. The Levites shall be mine,
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, lo! I have taken the Levites out of the midst of the sons of Israel, instead of every firstborn that a mother beareth, from among the sons of Israel,—so shall the Levites be mine;

text.NRSV: for all the firstborn are mine; when I killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated for my own all the firstborn in Israel, both human and animal; they shall be mine. I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: for, mine, is every firstborn, in the day when I smote every firstborn in the land of Egypt, I hallowed unto myself every firstborn in Israel, both of man and of beast,—mine, did they become, and belong unto me, Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, in the desert of Sinai, saying:—

text.NRSV: Enroll the Levites by ancestral houses and by clans. You shall enroll every male from a month old and upward.
text.Rotherham: Number thou the sons of Levi, by their ancestral houses, by their families,—every male, from one month old and upwards, shalt thou number them.

text.NRSV: So Moses enrolled them according to the word of the Lord, as he was commanded.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses numbered them according to the bidding of Yahweh,—as he was commanded.

text.NRSV: The following were the sons of Levi, by their names: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
text.Rotherham: And these were the sons of Levi, by their names,—Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the sons of Gershon by their clans: Libni and Shimei.
text.Rotherham: And these, the names of the sons of Gershon by their families,—Libni and Shimei.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath by their clans: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Kohath, by their families Amram, and Izhar, Hebron and Uzziel.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari by their clans: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites, by their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Merari by their families, Mahli and Mushi. These, are they—the families of the Levites by their ancestral houses.

text.NRSV: To Gershon belonged the clan of the Libnites and the clan of the Shimeites; these were the clans of the Gershonites.
text.Rotherham: To, Gershon, belonged the family of the Libnites, and the family of the Shimeites,—these, are they—the families of the Gershonites.

text.NRSV: Their enrollment, counting all the males from a month old and upward, was seven thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: They who were numbered of them in the counting of every male, from one month old, and upwards,—they who were num-bered of them, were seven thousand and five hundred.

text.NRSV: The clans of the Gershonites were to camp behind the tabernacle on the west,
text.Rotherham: the families of the Gershonites, to the rear of the habitation, were to encamp, westward.

text.NRSV: with Eliasaph son of Lael as head of the ancestral house of the Gershonites.
text.Rotherham: And, the prince of the ancestral house of the Gershonites, was Eliasaph son of Lael.

text.NRSV: The responsibility of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting was to be the tabernacle, the tent with its covering, the screen for the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: And, the charge, of the sons of Gershon, was over the tent of meeting, the habitation, and the tent,—the covering thereof, and the screen for the entrance of the tent of meeting;

text.NRSV: the hangings of the court, the screen for the entrance of the court that is around the tabernacle and the altar, and its cords—all the service pertaining to these.
text.Rotherham: and the hangings of the court, and the screen for the entrance of the court which was near the habitation and near the altar, round about,—and the cords thereof, as to all the laborious work thereof.

text.NRSV: To Kohath belonged the clan of the Amramites, the clan of the Izharites, the clan of the Hebronites, and the clan of the Uzzielites; these are the clans of the Kohathites.
text.Rotherham: And to Kohath, belonged the family of the Amramites and the family of the Izharites, and the family of the Hebronites, and the family of the Uzzielites,—these, are they—the families of the Kohathites.

text.NRSV: Counting all the males, from a month old and upward, there were eight thousand six hundred, attending to the duties of the sanctuary.
text.Rotherham: In, the counting of every male, from one month old and upwards, there were eight thousand and six hundred, to keep the charge of the sanctuary.

text.NRSV: The clans of the Kohathites were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle,
text.Rotherham: The, families of the sons of Kohath, were to encamp,—on the side of the habitation, south-ward.

text.NRSV: with Elizaphan son of Uzziel as head of the ancestral house of the clans of the Kohathites.
text.Rotherham: And the prince of the ancestral house of the families of the Kohathites, was Elizaphan, son of Uzziel.

text.NRSV: Their responsibility was the ark, the table, the lampstand, the altars, the vessels of the sanctuary with which the priests minister, and the screen—all the service pertaining to these.
text.Rotherham: And their charge, was—the ark and the table and the lampstand and the altars, and the utensils of the sanctuary wherewith they were to minister,—and the screen, as to all the laborious work thereof.

text.NRSV: Eleazar son of Aaron the priest was to be chief over the leaders of the Levites, and to have oversight of those who had charge of the sanctuary.
text.Rotherham: And the prince of the princes of the Levites, was Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest,—to have the oversight of them that were to keep the charge of the sanctuary.

text.NRSV: To Merari belonged the clan of the Mahlites and the clan of the Mushites; these are the clans of Merari.
text.Rotherham: To Merari, belonged the family of the Mahlites, and the family of the Mushites,—these, are they—the families of Merari.

text.NRSV: Their enrollment, counting all the males from a month old and upward, was six thousand two hundred.
text.Rotherham: And they who were numbered of them in the counting of every male, from one month old and upwards, were six thousand, and two hundred.

text.NRSV: The head of the ancestral house of the clans of Merari was Zuriel son of Abihail; they were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: And the prince of the ancestral house of the families of Merari, was Zuriel son of Abihail,—on the side of the habitation, were they to encamp northward.

text.NRSV: The appointed responsibility of the sons of Merari was the frames of the tabernacle, the bars, the pillars, the bases, and all their accessories—all the service pertaining to these;
text.Rotherham: And the oversight of the charge of the sons of Merari, was—the boards of the habitation, and the bars thereof and the pillars thereof and the sockets thereof,—and all the furniture thereof, as to all the laborious work thereof;

text.NRSV: also the pillars of the court all around, with their bases and pegs and cords.
text.Rotherham: also the pillars of the court round about and their sockets,—and their pins and their cords.

text.NRSV: Those who were to camp in front of the tabernacle on the east—in front of the tent of meeting toward the east—were Moses and Aaron and Aaron's sons, having charge of the rites within the sanctuary, whatever had to be done for the Israelites; and any outsider who came near was to be put to death.
text.Rotherham: But, they who were to encamp before the habitation eastwards before the tent of meeting, towards sunrise, were Moses and Aaron and his sons. to keep the charge of the sanctuary, as the charge of the sons of Israel and, the stranger that came near, was to be put to death.

text.NRSV: The total enrollment of the Levites whom Moses and Aaron enrolled at the commandment of the Lord, by their clans, all the males from a month old and upward, was twenty-two thousand.
text.Rotherham: All they who were numbered of the Levites whom Moses [and Aaron] numbered at the bidding of Yahweh by their families,—every male, from one month old and upwards, were two-and-twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses: Enroll all the firstborn males of the Israelites, from a month old and upward, and count their names.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses: Number thou every firstborn male belonging to the sons of Israel, from one month old and upwards,—and reckon up the number of their names;

text.NRSV: But you shall accept the Levites for me—I am the Lord—as substitutes for all the firstborn among the Israelites, and the livestock of the Levites as substitutes for all the firstborn among the livestock of the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou take the Levites for me, me Yahweh, instead of every firstborn among the sons of Israel,—also the cattle of the Levites, instead of every firstling among the cattle of the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: So Moses enrolled all the firstborn among the Israelites, as the Lord commanded him.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses numbered, as Yahweh commanded him,—every firstborn among the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: The total of all the firstborn males from a month old and upward, enrolled by name, was twenty-two thousand two hundred seventy-three.
text.Rotherham: And it was so—that all the firstborn of the males in the counting of names from one month old and upwards of such as were numbered of them,—were two-and-twenty thousand, two hundred and seventy-three.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:—

text.NRSV: Accept the Levites as substitutes for all the firstborn among the Israelites, and the livestock of the Levites as substitutes for their livestock; and the Levites shall be mine. I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Take the Levites, instead of all the firstborn among the sons of Israel, and the cattle of the Levites, instead of their cattle,—so shall the Levites belong unto me, me Yahweh;

text.NRSV: As the price of redemption of the two hundred seventy-three of the firstborn of the Israelites, over and above the number of the Levites,
text.Rotherham: and as the ransom of the two hundred and seventy-three,—who are more than the Levites, of the firstborn of the sons of Israel,

text.NRSV: you shall accept five shekels apiece, reckoning by the shekel of the sanctuary, a shekel of twenty gerahs.
text.Rotherham: thou shalt take five shekels apiece by the poll,—by the shekel of the sanctuary, shalt thou take it, twenty gerahs to the shekel;

text.NRSV: Give to Aaron and his sons the money with which the excess number of them is redeemed.
text.Rotherham: and shalt give the silver to Aaron and to his sons,—as the ransom of them that are in excess over them.

text.NRSV: So Moses took the redemption money from those who were over and above those redeemed by the Levites;
text.Rotherham: So Moses took the redemption silver from them who were in excess over them who were redeemed by the Levites:

text.NRSV: from the firstborn of the Israelites he took the money, one thousand three hundred sixty-five shekels, reckoned by the shekel of the sanctuary;
text.Rotherham: from the firstborn of the sons of Israel, took he the silver,—a thousand three hundred and sixty-five shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary,

text.NRSV: and Moses gave the redemption money to Aaron and his sons, according to the command of the Lord, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Moses gave the redemption silver to Aaron and to his sons at the bidding of Yahweh,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses and unto Aaron saying:

text.NRSV: Take a census of the Kohathites separate from the other Levites, by their clans and their ancestral houses,
text.Rotherham: Reckon up the sum of the sons of Kohath, out of the midst of the sons of Levi,—by their families by their ancestral house:

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old, all who qualify to do work relating to the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old, and upwards, even unto fifty years,—every one that can enter into the host, to do service in the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: The service of the Kohathites relating to the tent of meeting concerns the most holy things.
text.Rotherham: This, shall be the labour of the sons of Kohath, in the tent of meeting,—the most holy things,

text.NRSV: When the camp is to set out, Aaron and his sons shall go in and take down the screening curtain, and cover the ark of the covenant with it;
text.Rotherham: So then Aaron and his sons shall enter when the camp is to set forward, and take down the screening veil,—and enwrap therein the ark of the testimony;

text.NRSV: then they shall put on it a covering of fine leather, and spread over that a cloth all of blue, and shall put its poles in place.
text.Rotherham: and put thereon a covering of badgers’ skin, and spread a complete wrapper of blue over above,—and put in the staves thereof.

text.NRSV: Over the table of the bread of the Presence they shall spread a blue cloth, and put on it the plates, the dishes for incense, the bowls, and the flagons for the drink offering; the regular bread also shall be on it;
text.Rotherham: And over the Presence-table, shall they spread a wrapper of blue and put thereon the dishes and the pans and the bowls and the libation cups, the perpetual bread also, thereupon, shall remain;

text.NRSV: then they shall spread over them a crimson cloth, and cover it with a covering of fine leather, and shall put its poles in place.
text.Rotherham: and they shall spread over them a wrapper of crimson, and cover the same with a covering of badgers’ skin,—and shall put in the staves thereof.

text.NRSV: They shall take a blue cloth, and cover the lampstand for the light, with its lamps, its snuffers, its trays, and all the vessels for oil with which it is supplied;
text.Rotherham: Then shall they take a wrapper of blue, and cover the light-giving lampstand, and the lamps thereof, and the snuffers thereof and the snuff-trays thereof,—and all the oil-vessels, thereof, wherewith they minister thereunto;

text.NRSV: and they shall put it with all its utensils in a covering of fine leather, and put it on the carrying frame.
text.Rotherham: and shall put the same and all the vessels thereof, within a covering of, badgers’ skin,—and shall put it upon a frame.

text.NRSV: Over the golden altar they shall spread a blue cloth, and cover it with a covering of fine leather, and shall put its poles in place;
text.Rotherham: And upon the altar of gold, shall they spread a wrapper of blue, and shall cover the same in a covering of badgers’ skin,—and shall put in the staves thereof,

text.NRSV: and they shall take all the utensils of the service that are used in the sanctuary, and put them in a blue cloth, and cover them with a covering of fine leather, and put them on the carrying frame.
text.Rotherham: And they shall take all the utensils of attendance wherewith they attend in the sanctuary, and put them within a wrapper of blue, and cover the same in a covering of badgers’ skin,—and put them on a frame.

text.NRSV: They shall take away the ashes from the altar, and spread a purple cloth over it;
text.Rotherham: Then shall they remove the fat-ashes from the altar,—and spread over it a wrapper of purple;

text.NRSV: and they shall put on it all the utensils of the altar, which are used for the service there, the firepans, the forks, the shovels, and the basins, all the utensils of the altar; and they shall spread on it a covering of fine leather, and shall put its poles in place.
text.Rotherham: and put thereupon all the utensils thereof wherewith they minister thereupon—the fire-pans, the flesh-hooks—and the shovels and the tossing-bowls, all the utensils of the altar,—and shall spread over it a covering of badgers’ skin and put in the staves thereof.

text.NRSV: When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the furnishings of the sanctuary, as the camp sets out, after that the Kohathites shall come to carry these, but they must not touch the holy things, or they will die. These are the things of the tent of meeting that the Kohathites are to carry.
text.Rotherham: So shall Aaron and his sons make an end of covering the sanctuary, and all the utensils of the sanctuary, when the camp is to set forward, then after that, shall the sons of Kohath enter to bear it, but they must not put forth a touch unto that which, is holy, else should they die, these, shall be the burden of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: Eleazar son of Aaron the priest shall have charge of the oil for the light, the fragrant incense, the regular grain offering, and the anointing oil, the supervision of all the tabernacle and all that is in it, in the sanctuary and in its utensils.
text.Rotherham: But the care of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, [shall be] —the light-giving oil, and the fragrant incense, and the perpetual meal-offering, and the anointing oil, the care of all the habitation, and all that is therein, both as to the sanctuary, and as to the furniture thereof.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying:

text.NRSV: You must not let the tribe of the clans of the Kohathites be destroyed from among the Levites.
text.Rotherham: Do not let the tribe of the families of the Kohathites be cut off out of the midst of the Levites.

text.NRSV: This is how you must deal with them in order that they may live and not die when they come near to the most holy things: Aaron and his sons shall go in and assign each to a particular task or burden.
text.Rotherham: This, therefore, do ye for them, so shall they live and not die when they approach the most holy place,—Aaron and his sons shall enter and set them man by man over his labour, and unto his burden;

text.NRSV: But the Kohathites must not go in to look on the holy things even for a moment; otherwise they will die.
text.Rotherham: but they shall not enter to see, for a moment, that which is holy, else should they die.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Take a census of the Gershonites also, by their ancestral houses and by their clans;
text.Rotherham: reckon up the sum of the sons of Gershon even of them also,—by their ancestral house by their families;

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old you shall enroll them, all who qualify to do work in the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards even to fifty years, shalt thou number them,—All that may enter to take rank in the host, to do laborious work in the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: This is the service of the clans of the Gershonites, in serving and bearing burdens:
text.Rotherham: This, shall be the labour of the families of the Gershonites,—in labouring and in bearing:

text.NRSV: they shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle, and the tent of meeting with its covering, and the outer covering of fine leather that is on top of it, and the screen for the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: they shall bear the curtains of the habitation and the tent of meeting, the covering thereof and the covering of badgers’ skin which is over above it,—and the screen, at the entrance of the tent of meeting;

text.NRSV: and the hangings of the court, and the screen for the entrance of the gate of the court that is around the tabernacle and the altar, and their cords, and all the equipment for their service; and they shall do all that needs to be done with regard to them.
text.Rotherham: and the hangings of the court, and the screen for the entrance of the gate of the court which is near the habitation and near the altar round about, and their cords, and all their articles of service, and all that is to be done to them when they shall do their labour,

text.NRSV: All the service of the Gershonites shall be at the command of Aaron and his sons, in all that they are to carry, and in all that they have to do; and you shall assign to their charge all that they are to carry.
text.Rotherham: At the bidding of Aaron and his sons, shall be all the labour of the sons of the Gershonites, to the extent of their every burden, and to the extent of all their labour,—so shall ye appoint unto them in charge their every burden.

text.NRSV: This is the service of the clans of the Gershonites in the tent of meeting, and their duties are to be under the oversight of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
text.Rotherham: This, shall be the labour of the families of the sons of the Gershonites in the tent of meeting,—and, the charge of them, shall be in the hand of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.

text.NRSV: As for the Merarites, you shall enroll them by their clans and their ancestral houses;
text.Rotherham: As for the sons of Merari,—by their families by their ancestral house, shall ye number them;

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old you shall enroll them, everyone who qualifies to do the work of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards even to fifty years, shall ye number them,—all that enter into the host, to do laborious work in the tent of meeting,

text.NRSV: This is what they are charged to carry, as the whole of their service in the tent of meeting: the frames of the tabernacle, with its bars, pillars, and bases,
text.Rotherham: And, this, shall be their charge of the burdens, to the extent of all their labour in the tent of meeting, the boards of the habitation, and the bars thereof and he pillars thereof and the sockets thereof:

text.NRSV: and the pillars of the court all around with their bases, pegs, and cords, with all their equipment and all their related service; and you shall assign by name the objects that they are required to carry.
text.Rotherham: and the pillars of the court round about and their sockets and their pins and their cords, to the extent of all their articles, to the extent of all their labour, and by names, shall ye put under their care the articles of their charge of burdens.

text.NRSV: This is the service of the clans of the Merarites, the whole of their service in the tent of meeting, under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
text.Rotherham: This, shall be the labour of the families of the sons of Merari, to the extent of all their labour in the tent of meeting,—in the hand of Ithamar, son of Aaron, the priest.

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron and the leaders of the congregation enrolled the Kohathites, by their clans and their ancestral houses,
text.Rotherham: Then did Moses and Aaron, and the princes of the assembly, number the sons of the Kohathites, by their families, and by their ancestral house;

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old, everyone who qualified for work relating to the tent of meeting;
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards, even unto fifty years old, all that might enter the host, to labour in the tent of meeting;

text.NRSV: and their enrollment by clans was two thousand seven hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: and they who were numbered of them, by their families, were found to be two thousand, seven hundred and fifty.

text.NRSV: This was the enrollment of the clans of the Kohathites, all who served at the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron enrolled according to the commandment of the Lord by Moses.
text.Rotherham: These, were they who were num-bered of the families of the Kohathites, all that might labour in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron did number, at the bidding of Yahweh by the mediation of Moses.

text.NRSV: The enrollment of the Gershonites, by their clans and their ancestral houses,
text.Rotherham: And they who were numbered of the sons of Gershon,—by their families, and by their ancestral house;

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old, everyone who qualified for work relating to the tent of meeting—
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards, even to fifty years old, all that might enter the host, to labour in the tent of meeting,:

text.NRSV: their enrollment by their clans and their ancestral houses was two thousand six hundred thirty.
text.Rotherham: yea they who were numbered of them, by their families, by their ancestral house,—were found to be—two thousand, and six hundred, and thirty.

text.NRSV: This was the enrollment of the clans of the Gershonites, all who served at the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron enrolled according to the commandment of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who were numbered of the families of the sons of Gershon, all who might labour in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron did number, at the bidding of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The enrollment of the clans of the Merarites, by their clans and their ancestral houses,
text.Rotherham: And they who were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari, by their families by their ancestral house;

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old, everyone who qualified for work relating to the tent of meeting—
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards, even unto fifty years old,—all that might enter the host to labour in the tent of meeting;

text.NRSV: their enrollment by their clans was three thousand two hundred.
text.Rotherham: yea they who were numbered of them, by their families, were found to be—three thousand and two hundred.

text.NRSV: This is the enrollment of the clans of the Merarites, whom Moses and Aaron enrolled according to the commandment of the Lord by Moses.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari,—whom Moses and Aaron did number, at the bidding of Yahweh, by the mediation of Moses.

text.NRSV: All those who were enrolled of the Levites, whom Moses and Aaron and the leaders of Israel enrolled, by their clans and their ancestral houses,
text.Rotherham: All they who were numbered, whom Moses and Aaron and the princes of Israel did number, even the Levites, by their families and by their ancestral house;

text.NRSV: from thirty to fifty years old, everyone who qualified to do the work of service and the work of bearing burdens relating to the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards, even unto fifty years old, all that might enter, to toil in the work of labouring and in the work of bearing burdens, in the tent of meeting,

text.NRSV: their enrollment was eight thousand five hundred eighty.
text.Rotherham: yea, they who were numbered of them were found to be—eight thousand, and five hundred, and eighty.

text.NRSV: According to the commandment of the Lord through Moses they were appointed to their several tasks of serving or carrying; thus they were enrolled by him, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: At the bidding of Yahweh, were they put in charge by the mediation of Moses, man by man, over his labour and over his burden,—yea, they who were put in charge by him, were they, whom Yahweh commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:—

text.NRSV: Command the Israelites to put out of the camp everyone who is leprous, or has a discharge, and everyone who is unclean through contact with a corpse;
text.Rotherham: Command the sons of Israel, that they send forth out of the camp, every leper, and every one that hath a flux,—and every one that is unclean by the dead:

text.NRSV: you shall put out both male and female, putting them outside the camp; they must not defile their camp, where I dwell among them.
text.Rotherham: whether male or female, ye shall send them forth, unto the outside of the camp, shall ye send them,—that they make not their camps unclean, in the midst whereof, I, have my habitation.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did so, putting them outside the camp; as the Lord had spoken to Moses, so the Israelites did.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel did so, and sent them forth unto the outside of the camp,—as Yahweh spake unto Moses, so, did the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites: When a man or a woman wrongs another, breaking faith with the Lord, that person incurs guilt
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel: When any man or woman, shall do aught of any human sin, in acting unfaithfully against Yahweh,—and that person shall become aware of his guilt,

text.NRSV: and shall confess the sin that has been committed. The person shall make full restitution for the wrong, adding one fifth to it, and giving it to the one who was wronged.
text.Rotherham: then shall they confess their sin which they have done, and he shall make good that wherein he is guilty, in the principal thereof, and the fifth part thereof, shall he add thereunto,—and give to him against whom he is guilty,

text.NRSV: If the injured party has no next of kin to whom restitution may be made for the wrong, the restitution for wrong shall go to the Lord for the priest, in addition to the ram of atonement with which atonement is made for the guilty party.
text.Rotherham: But if one have no kinsman unto whom he may make good that wherein he is guilty, then that wherein he is guilty, which is to be restored to Yahweh, shall be the priest’s,—besides the ram of propitiation, wherewith a propitiatory-covering is to be put over him.

text.NRSV: Among all the sacred donations of the Israelites, every gift that they bring to the priest shall be his.
text.Rotherham: And every heave-offering, of all the hallowed things of the sons of Israel which they bring near to the priest, unto him, shall it belong.

text.NRSV: The sacred donations of all are their own; whatever anyone gives to the priest shall be his.
text.Rotherham: And, every man’s hallowed things, shall be, his own,—what any man giveth to the priest, shall be his.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: If any man's wife goes astray and is unfaithful to him,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—When any man’s wife, shall turn aside, and commit against him an act of unfaithfulness;

text.NRSV: if a man has had intercourse with her but it is hidden from her husband, so that she is undetected though she has defiled herself, and there is no witness against her since she was not caught in the act;
text.Rotherham: and a man shall lie with her carnally, and it shall be concealed from the eyes of her husband and be kept close but, she, hath committed uncleanness,—though witness, there is none against her, and she, hath not been caught;

text.NRSV: if a spirit of jealousy comes on him, and he is jealous of his wife who has defiled herself; or if a spirit of jealousy comes on him, and he is jealous of his wife, though she has not defiled herself;
text.Rotherham: but there shall pass over him a spirit of jealousy, and he shall become jealous of his wife, she having committed uncleanness,—or there shall pass over him a spirit of jealousy, and he shall become jealous of his wife, she not, having committed uncleanness,

text.NRSV: then the man shall bring his wife to the priest, and bring the offering required for her, one tenth of an ephah of barley flour. He shall pour no oil on it and put no frankincense on it, for it is a grain offering of jealousy, a grain offering of remembrance, bringing iniquity to remembrance.
text.Rotherham: then shall the man bring in his wife unto the priest, and shall bring in her offering for her, the tenth of an ephah of the meal of barley,—he shall not pour thereon oil, nor put thereon frankincense, for a jealousy gift, it is, a reminding gift bringing to mind iniquity.

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall bring her near, and set her before the Lord;
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest bring her near,—and cause her to stand before Yahweh;

text.NRSV: the priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel, and take some of the dust that is on the floor of the tabernacle and put it into the water.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall take hallowed water in an earthen vessel,—and of the dust which shall be upon the floor of the habitation, shall the priest take, and put into the water;

text.NRSV: The priest shall set the woman before the Lord, dishevel the woman's head, put the grain offering of remembrance in her hands, which is the grain offering of jealousy. In his own hand the priest shall have the water of bitterness that brings the curse.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall cause the woman to stand before Yahweh, and shall bare the head of the woman, and shall place upon her hands the reminding gift, it being a jealousy gift,—and in the hand of the priest, shall be the deadly water that bringeth a curse;

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall make her take an oath, saying, "If no man has lain with you, if you have not turned aside to uncleanness while under your husband's authority, be immune to this water of bitterness that brings the curse.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall put her on oath and shall say unto the woman:—If no man hath lain with thee, and if thou hast not turned aside in uncleanness, [unto another] instead of thy husband, be thou clear from this deadly water that causeth a curse.

text.NRSV: But if you have gone astray while under your husband's authority, if you have defiled yourself and some man other than your husband has had intercourse with you"
text.Rotherham: But, if, thou, hast turned aside, [to another] instead of thy husband and if thou hast made thyself unclean,—in that a man hath known thee carnally, other than thy husband,

text.NRSV: —let the priest make the woman take the oath of the curse and say to the woman—"the Lord make you an execration and an oath among your people, when the Lord makes your uterus drop, your womb discharge;
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest put the woman on oath with an oath of cursing, and the priest shall say unto the woman, Yahweh give thee up for a curse and for an oath in the midst of thy people,—in that Yahweh shall give up thy thigh to fall away, and thy womb to swell:

text.NRSV: now may this water that brings the curse enter your bowels and make your womb discharge, your uterus drop!" And the woman shall say, "Amen. Amen."
text.Rotherham: so shall this water that causeth a curse enter into thy body, causing womb to swell and thigh to fall away. And the woman shall say, Amen, Amen.

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall put these curses in writing, and wash them off into the water of bitterness.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest write these curses in a scroll,—and wipe them out into the deadly water;

text.NRSV: He shall make the woman drink the water of bitterness that brings the curse, and the water that brings the curse shall enter her and cause bitter pain.
text.Rotherham: and shall cause the woman to drink the deadly water that causeth a curse,—and the deadly water that causeth a curse shall enter into her.

text.NRSV: The priest shall take the grain offering of jealousy out of the woman's hand, and shall elevate the grain offering before the Lord and bring it to the altar;
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest take from the hand of the woman the jealousy meal-offering,—and shall wave the meal-offering before Yahweh, and bring it near unto the altar;

text.NRSV: and the priest shall take a handful of the grain offering, as its memorial portion, and turn it into smoke on the altar, and afterward shall make the woman drink the water.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall take a handful from the meal-offering, a remembrancer thereof, and make a perfume at the altar,—and afterwards, shall cause the woman to drink the water.

text.NRSV: When he has made her drink the water, then, if she has defiled herself and has been unfaithful to her husband, the water that brings the curse shall enter into her and cause bitter pain, and her womb shall discharge, her uterus drop, and the woman shall become an execration among her people.
text.Rotherham: And as soon as he causeth her to drink, the water, then shall it be, that, if she have fallen into uncleanness and committed unfaithfulness against her husband, as soon as the deadly water that causeth a curse hath entered into her, so soon shall her womb swell and her thigh fall away,—thus shall the woman become a curse in the midst of her people.

text.NRSV: But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean, then she shall be immune and be able to conceive children.
text.Rotherham: But, if the woman have not fallen into uncleanness, but is pure, then shall she be clear and shall bear seed.

text.NRSV: This is the law in cases of jealousy, when a wife, while under her husband's authority, goes astray and defiles herself,
text.Rotherham: This, is the law of jealousies,—when a wife shall turn aside [to another] instead of her husband, and fall into uncleanness;

text.NRSV: or when a spirit of jealousy comes on a man and he is jealous of his wife; then he shall set the woman before the Lord, and the priest shall apply this entire law to her.
text.Rotherham: or when there passeth over, a husband, a spirit of jealousy, and he becometh jealous of his wife, then shall he cause the woman to stand before Yahweh, and the priest shall execute upon her all this law:

text.NRSV: The man shall be free from iniquity, but the woman shall bear her iniquity.
text.Rotherham: thus shall the man be clear of iniquity; but that woman, shall bear her iniquity.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When either men or women make a special vow, the vow of a nazirite, to separate themselves to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—When any man or woman, would make the special vow of One Separate, by separating himself unto Yahweh,

text.NRSV: they shall separate themselves from wine and strong drink; they shall drink no wine vinegar or other vinegar, and shall not drink any grape juice or eat grapes, fresh or dried.
text.Rotherham: from wine and strong drink, shall he separate himself, neither vinegar of wine nor vinegar of strong drink, shall he drink,—and no liquor of grapes, shall he drink, no grapes fresh or dried, shall he eat.

text.NRSV: All their days as nazirites they shall eat nothing that is produced by the grapevine, not even the seeds or the skins.
text.Rotherham: All the days of his separation, of nothing that is made from the grapevine from the seeds even to the skin, shall he eat.

text.NRSV: All the days of their nazirite vow no razor shall come upon the head; until the time is completed for which they separate themselves to the Lord, they shall be holy; they shall let the locks of the head grow long.
text.Rotherham: All the days of his vow of separation, no razor, shall pass over his head,—until the days are fulfilled for which he shall separate himself to Yahweh hallowed, shall he be, letting the locks of the hair of his head grow long.

text.NRSV: All the days that they separate themselves to the Lord they shall not go near a corpse.
text.Rotherham: All the days for which he hath separated himself unto Yahweh to no dead person, shall he go in.

text.NRSV: Even if their father or mother, brother or sister, should die, they may not defile themselves; because their consecration to God is upon the head.
text.Rotherham: Neither for his father nor for his mother nor for his brother nor for his sister, shall he make himself unclean-not even for them, should they die,—because, his separation unto God, is upon his head,

text.NRSV: All their days as nazirites they are holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: All his days of separation hallowed, is he unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: If someone dies very suddenly nearby, defiling the consecrated head, then they shall shave the head on the day of their cleansing; on the seventh day they shall shave it.
text.Rotherham: But if one that is dying should die by him in a moment suddenly, then shall he count unclean his head of separation,—and shall shave his head, on the day he cleanseth himself, on the seventh day, shall he shave it.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day they shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: And on the eighth day, shall he bring in. two turtle-doves, or two young pigeons,—unto the priest, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting;

text.NRSV: and the priest shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, and make atonement for them, because they incurred guilt by contact with a corpse. They shall sanctify the head that same day,
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall make, of one a sin—bearer and of one an ascending-sacrifice, and so put a propitiatory-covering over him, for that he sinned in respect of the dead person,—thus shall he hallow his head on that day.

text.NRSV: and separate themselves to the Lord for their days as nazirites, and bring a male lamb in its first year as a guilt offering. The former time shall be void, because the consecrated head was defiled.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he separate unto Yahweh his days of separation, and shall bring in a he-lamb a year old as a guilt-bearer,—and, the first days, shall be lost because his separation, was made unclean.

text.NRSV: This is the law for nazirites when the time of their consecration has been completed: they shall be brought to the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the law of the Separate One, on the day when he fulfilleth his days of separation, he shall be brought in unto the entrance of the tent of meeting:

text.NRSV: and they shall offer their gift to the Lord, one male lamb in its first year without blemish as a burnt offering, one ewe lamb in its first year without blemish as a sin offering, one ram without blemish as an offering of well-being,
text.Rotherham: then shall he bring near as his offering unto Yahweh.—one he-lamb a year old, without defect for an ascending-sacrifice, and one ewe-lamb a year old without defect for a sin-bearer,—and one ram, without defect for a peace-offering;

text.NRSV: and a basket of unleavened bread, cakes of choice flour mixed with oil and unleavened wafers spread with oil, with their grain offering and their drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: and a basket of unleavened cakes—fine meal in round cakes overflowed with oil, and thin cakes of unleavened bread, anointed with oil,—with their meal-offering and their drink-offerings.

text.NRSV: The priest shall present them before the Lord and offer their sin offering and burnt offering,
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest bring [them] near before Yahweh,—and offer his sin-bearer and his ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: and shall offer the ram as a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord, with the basket of unleavened bread; the priest also shall make the accompanying grain offering and drink offering.
text.Rotherham: and the ram, shall he offer as a peace-offering unto Yahweh, besides the basket of unleavened cakes,—and the priest shall offer the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof,

text.NRSV: Then the nazirites shall shave the consecrated head at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and shall take the hair from the consecrated head and put it on the fire under the sacrifice of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the Separate One shave, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, his head of separation,—and take the hair of his head of separation, and put upon the fire, which is under the peace-offering.

text.NRSV: The priest shall take the shoulder of the ram, when it is boiled, and one unleavened cake out of the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and shall put them in the palms of the nazirites, after they have shaved the consecrated head.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest take the shoulder for boiling from the ram, and one unleavened round cake from the basket, and one unleavened thin cake,—and place them on the hands, of the Separate One after he hath shaven off his [hair of] separation;

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall elevate them as an elevation offering before the Lord; they are a holy portion for the priest, together with the breast that is elevated and the thigh that is offered. After that the nazirites may drink wine.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall wave them as a wave-offering before Yahweh, hallowed, it is for the priest, besides the wave breast, and besides the heave leg,—and, afterwards, may the Separate One drink wine.

text.NRSV: This is the law for the nazirites who take a vow. Their offering to the Lord must be in accordance with the nazirite vow, apart from what else they can afford. In accordance with whatever vow they take, so they shall do, following the law for their consecration.
text.Rotherham: This is the law of One Separate what he shall vow, his offering unto Yahweh with respect to his separation, besides what his hand may obtain: as required by the vow that he shall vow, so, must he do, with respect to the law of his separation.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying, Thus you shall bless the Israelites: You shall say to them,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons saying Thus, shall ye bless the sons of Israel,—saying unto them:

text.NRSV: The Lord bless you and keep you;
text.Rotherham: Yahweh bless thee, and keep thee:

text.NRSV: the Lord make his face to shine upon you, and be gracious to you;
text.Rotherham: Yahweh cause his face to shine upon thee and be gracious unto thee:

text.NRSV: the Lord lift up his countenance upon you, and give you peace.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh lift up his face unto thee, and appoint unto thee peace.

text.NRSV: So they shall put my name on the Israelites, and I will bless them.
text.Rotherham: Thus shall they put my name upon the sons of Israel,—and, I, myself, will bless them.

text.NRSV: On the day when Moses had finished setting up the tabernacle, and had anointed and consecrated it with all its furnishings, and had anointed and consecrated the altar with all its utensils,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the day when Moses made an end of rearing up the habitation and anointing it, and hallowing it, and all the furniture thereof, and the altar, and all the utensils thereof,—both of anointing them, and hallowing them,

text.NRSV: the leaders of Israel, heads of their ancestral houses (the leaders of the tribes, those who were over the enrolled),
text.Rotherham: that the princes of Israel, the heads of their ancestral houses, made an offering,—they being princes of the tribes, they being the men who stood over them who were numbered:

text.NRSV: brought their offerings before the Lord, six covered wagons and twelve oxen, a wagon for every two of the leaders, and for each one an ox; they presented them before the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: yea then brought they in as their offering, before Yahweh—six covered waggons, and twelve oxen, a waggon for every two of the princes and an ox for each one,—yea they brought them near before the habitation.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Accept these from them, that they may be used in doing the service of the tent of meeting, and give them to the Levites, to each according to his service.
text.Rotherham: Take [them] of them, so shall they be for doing the laborious work of the tent of meeting,—and thou shalt give them unto the Levites, to every man according to the need of his laborious work.

text.NRSV: So Moses took the wagons and the oxen, and gave them to the Levites.
text.Rotherham: So Moses took the waggons, and the oxen,—and gave them unto the Levites:

text.NRSV: Two wagons and four oxen he gave to the Gershonites, according to their service;
text.Rotherham: two of the waggons and four of the oxen, gave he unto the sons of Gershon,—according to the need of their laborious work;

text.NRSV: and four wagons and eight oxen he gave to the Merarites, according to their service, under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
text.Rotherham: and four of the waggons, and eight of the oxen, gave he unto the sons of Merari,—according to the need of their laborious work, in the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest;

text.NRSV: But to the Kohathites he gave none, because they were charged with the care of the holy things that had to be carried on the shoulders.
text.Rotherham: but, unto the sons of Kohath, gave he none,—because as to the laborious work of the sanctuary which pertained unto them, upon their shoulders, were they to carry it.

text.NRSV: The leaders also presented offerings for the dedication of the altar at the time when it was anointed; the leaders presented their offering before the altar.
text.Rotherham: So then the princes offered for the dedication of the altar, on the day it was anointed,—yea the princes offered their oblation before the altar,

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses: They shall present their offerings, one leader each day, for the dedication of the altar.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses: Each prince on his appointed day, thus shall they offer their oblation, for the dedication of the altar.

text.NRSV: The one who presented his offering the first day was Nahshon son of Amminadab, of the tribe of Judah;
text.Rotherham: And so it was, that, he who on the first day, offered his oblation,—was Nahshon son of Amminadab of the tribe of Judah;

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: and his offering, was—one charger of silver, a hundred and thirty shekels, the weight thereof, one tossing bowl of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil for a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten shekels of gold full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock one ram one he-lamb of the first year, as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.
text.Rotherham: and as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Nashon, son of Amminadab.

text.NRSV: On the second day Nethanel son of Zuar, the leader of Issachar, presented an offering;
text.Rotherham: On the second day, Nethanel, son of Zuar, prince of Issachar, brought his offering.

text.NRSV: he presented for his offering one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: He offered as his oblation—one charger of silver, a hundred and thirty shekels, the weight thereof, one tossing bowl of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten [shekels] of gold full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock one ram one he-lamb of the first year, as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat as a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nethanel son of Zuar.
text.Rotherham: and, as peace-offering two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Nethanel, son of Zuar.

text.NRSV: On the third day Eliab son of Helon, the leader of the Zebulunites:
text.Rotherham: On the third day, the prince of the sons of Zebulun,—Eliab, son of Helon:

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: his offering,—one charger of silver a hundred and thirty shekels, the weight thereof, one tossing bowl of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon, of ten [shekels] of gold full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
