text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock, one ram, one he-lamb of the first year, as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliab son of Helon.
text.Rotherham: and as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Eliab, son of Helon.

text.NRSV: On the fourth day Elizur son of Shedeur, the leader of the Reubenites:
text.Rotherham: On the fourth day, the prince of he sons of Reuben,—Elizur, son of Shedeur:

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: his offering, one charger of silver, a hundred and thirty [shekels], the weight thereof, one tossing bowl of Silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil, as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten [shekels] of gold full of incense:

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock one ram, one he-lamb of the first year, as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat for a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Elizur son of Shedeur.
text.Rotherham: and as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Elizur, son of Shedeur.

text.NRSV: On the fifth day Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai, the leader of the Simeonites:
text.Rotherham: On the fifth day, the prince of the sons of Simeon,—Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai:

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: his offering, one charger of silver a hundred and thirty shekels, the weight thereof, one tossing bowl of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten [shekels] of gold, full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock one ram one he-lamb of the first year as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
text.Rotherham: and, as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai.

text.NRSV: On the sixth day Eliasaph son of Deuel, the leader of the Gadites:
text.Rotherham: On the sixth day, the prince of the sons of Gad,—Eliasaph, son of Deuel:

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: his offering, one charger of silver, a hundred and thirty [shekels], the weight thereof, one tossing bowl, of silver, seventy shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil, as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten [shekels] of gold full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock one ram one he-lamb of the first year, as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliasaph son of Deuel.
text.Rotherham: and as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Eliasaph, son of Deuel.

text.NRSV: On the seventh day Elishama son of Ammihud, the leader of the Ephraimites:
text.Rotherham: On the seventh day, the prince of the sons of Ephraim,—Elishama, son of Ammihud:

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: his offering, one charger of silver a hundred and thirty shekels, the weight thereof, one tossing bowl of silver, seventy shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten [shekels] of gold, full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock, one ram, one he-lamb of the first year, as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Elishama son of Ammihud.
text.Rotherham: and as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Elishama, son of Ammihud.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day Gamaliel son of Pedahzur, the leader of the Manassites:
text.Rotherham: On the eighth day, the prince of the sons of Manasseh,—Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur:

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: his offering, one charger of silver, a hundred and thirty [shekels], the weight thereof, one tossing bowl, of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten [shekels] of gold full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock one ram one he-lamb of the first year as an ascending-sacrifice:

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
text.Rotherham: and as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.

text.NRSV: On the ninth day Abidan son of Gideoni, the leader of the Benjaminites:
text.Rotherham: On the ninth day, the prince of the sons of Benjamin,—Abidan, son of Gideoni:

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: his offering, one charger of silver, a hundred and thirty [shekels], the weight thereof,—one tossing bowl of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil, as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon, of ten [shekels] of gold full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock, one ram, one he-lamb of the first year as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Abidan son of Gideoni.
text.Rotherham: and as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Abidan, son of Gideoni.

text.NRSV: On the tenth day Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai, the leader of the Danites:
text.Rotherham: On the tenth day, the prince of the sons of Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai:

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: his offering, one charger of silver a hundred and thirty [shekels], the weight thereof,—one tossing bowl of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon, of ten [shekels] of gold, full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock one ram one he-lamb of the first year as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
text.Rotherham: and as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Ahiezer, son of Ammishaddai.

text.NRSV: On the eleventh day Pagiel son of Ochran, the leader of the Asherites:
text.Rotherham: On the eleventh day, the prince of the sons of Asher, Pagiel son of Ochran;

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: his offering, one charger of silver, a hundred and thirty [shekels], the weight thereof, one tossing bowl, of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten [shekels] of gold, full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock, one ram one he-lamb of the first year, as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Pagiel son of Ochran.
text.Rotherham: and as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Pagiel, son of Ochran.

text.NRSV: On the twelfth day Ahira son of Enan, the leader of the Naphtalites:
text.Rotherham: On the twelfth day, the prince of the sons of Naphtali, Ahira, son of Enan:

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: his offering, one charger of silver a hundred and thirty [shekels], the weight thereof, one tossing bowl of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil, as a meal-offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten [shekels] of gold full of incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock one ram one he-lamb of the first year, as an ascending-sacrifice;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat as a sin-bearer;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Ahira son of Enan.
text.Rotherham: and, as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year, this, was the offering of Ahira, son of Enan.

text.NRSV: This was the dedication offering for the altar, on the day when it was anointed, from the leaders of Israel: twelve silver plates, twelve silver basins, twelve golden dishes,
text.Rotherham: This, was the offering for the dedication of the altar in the day when it was anointed, from the princes of Israel,—twelve chargers of silver twelve tossing bowls of silver, twelve spoons of gold;

text.NRSV: each silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels and each basin seventy, all the silver of the vessels two thousand four hundred shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary,
text.Rotherham: a hundred and thirty [shekels], each charger of silver, and seventy, each tossing bowl,—all the silver of the vessels, two thousand and four hundred [shekels], by the shekel of the sanctuary;

text.NRSV: the twelve golden dishes, full of incense, weighing ten shekels apiece according to the shekel of the sanctuary, all the gold of the dishes one hundred twenty shekels;
text.Rotherham: twelve spoons of gold full of incense, ten shekels each spoon by the shekel of the sanctuary,—all the gold of the spoons, a hundred and twenty [shekels] .

text.NRSV: all the livestock for the burnt offering twelve bulls, twelve rams, twelve male lambs a year old, with their grain offering; and twelve male goats for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: All the herd for the ascending-sacrifice,—twelve bullocks twelve rams twelve he-lambs of the first year with their meal-offering,—twelve young he-goats for the bearing of sin;

text.NRSV: and all the livestock for the sacrifice of well-being twenty-four bulls, the rams sixty, the male goats sixty, the male lambs a year old sixty. This was the dedication offering for the altar, after it was anointed.
text.Rotherham: and all the herd for the peace-offering, twenty-four bullocks sixty rams, sixty he-goats sixty he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering for the dedication of the altar, after it was anointed.

text.NRSV: When Moses went into the tent of meeting to speak with the Lord, he would hear the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat that was on the ark of the covenant from between the two cherubim; thus it spoke to him.
text.Rotherham: And when Moses entered into the tent of meeting, that He might speak with him, then heard he the Voice speaking unto him from off the propitiatory that was upon the ark of the testimony, from between the two cherubim,—so He spake unto him.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and say to him: When you set up the lamps, the seven lamps shall give light in front of the lampstand.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and thou shalt say unto him,—When thou lightest up the lamps, over against the front of the lampstand, shall the seven lamps give light.

text.NRSV: Aaron did so; he set up its lamps to give light in front of the lampstand, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron did so, over against the front of the lampstand, lighted he up the lamps thereof,—As Yahweh commanded Moses,

text.NRSV: Now this was how the lampstand was made, out of hammered work of gold. From its base to its flowers, it was hammered work; according to the pattern that the Lord had shown Moses, so he made the lampstand.
text.Rotherham: Now, this, was the work of the lampstand—beaten work of gold both in the shaft thereof and in the flowers thereof, was it beaten work, according to the appearance which Yahweh caused to appear unto Moses, so, made he the lampstand.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Separate the Levites from among the Israelites, and cleanse them.
text.Rotherham: Take the Levites, out of the midst of the sons of Israel,—and thou shalt purify them.

text.NRSV: Thus you shall do to them, to cleanse them: sprinkle the water of purification on them, have them shave their whole body with a razor and wash their clothes, and so cleanse themselves.
text.Rotherham: And, thus, shalt thou do unto them, to purify them, sprinkle upon them sin-cleansing water,—then shall they, cause a razor to pass over all their flesh, and shall wash their clothes and so make themselves pure.

text.NRSV: Then let them take a young bull and its grain offering of choice flour mixed with oil, and you shall take another young bull for a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: Then shall they take a choice young bullock, with the meal-offering thereof even fine meal overflowed with oil,—and a second choice young bullock, shalt thou take. as a sin-bearer.

text.NRSV: You shall bring the Levites before the tent of meeting, and assemble the whole congregation of the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou bring the Levites near, before the tent of meeting,—and shalt call together all the assembly of the sons of Israel;

text.NRSV: When you bring the Levites before the Lord, the Israelites shall lay their hands on the Levites,
text.Rotherham: and shalt bring the Levites near before Yahweh,—and the sons of Israel shall lean their hands upon the Levites;

text.NRSV: and Aaron shall present the Levites before the Lord as an elevation offering from the Israelites, that they may do the service of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and Aaron shall offer the Levites as a wave-offering before Yahweh, from among the sons of Israel,—so shall they be for performing the laborious work of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The Levites shall lay their hands on the heads of the bulls, and he shall offer the one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering to the Lord, to make atonement for the Levites.
text.Rotherham: And, the Levites, shall lean their hands upon the head of the bullocks; then make thou of the one a sin-bearer and of the other an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh, to put a propitiatory-covering over the Levites.

text.NRSV: Then you shall have the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons, and you shall present them as an elevation offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thus shalt thou cause the Levites to stand before Aaron and before his sons,—thus shalt thou offer them as a wave-offering unto Yahweh;

text.NRSV: Thus you shall separate the Levites from among the other Israelites, and the Levites shall be mine.
text.Rotherham: thus shalt thou separate the Levites out of the midst of the sons of Israel,—thus shall the Levites become mine.

text.NRSV: Thereafter the Levites may go in to do service at the tent of meeting, once you have cleansed them and presented them as an elevation offering.
text.Rotherham: And after that, shall the Levites enter to do the labour of the tent of meeting,—so shalt thou purify them, and offer them as a wave-offering,

text.NRSV: For they are unreservedly given to me from among the Israelites; I have taken them for myself, in place of all that open the womb, the firstborn of all the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: For, given, given, they are unto me, out of the midst of the sons of Israel,—instead of every firstborn that a mother beareth from among the sons of Israel, have I taken them unto me.

text.NRSV: For all the firstborn among the Israelites are mine, both human and animal. On the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt I consecrated them for myself,
text.Rotherham: For mine, is every firstborn among the sons of Israel, among men and among beasts,—on the day when I smote every firstborn in the land of Egypt, did I hallow them unto me.

text.NRSV: but I have taken the Levites in place of all the firstborn among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Therefore have I taken the Levites,—instead of every firstborn among the sons of Israel;

text.NRSV: Moreover, I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the Israelites, to do the service for the Israelites at the tent of meeting, and to make atonement for the Israelites, in order that there may be no plague among the Israelites for coming too close to the sanctuary.
text.Rotherham: therefore have I given the Levites as a gift unto Aaron and unto his sons, out of the midst of the sons of Israel, to do the laborious work of the sons of Israel in the tent of meeting, and to put a propitiatory-covering over the sons of Israel,—so shall there be among the sons of Israel, no plague, by the coming nigh of the sons of Israel unto the sanctuary.

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron and the whole congregation of the Israelites did with the Levites accordingly; the Israelites did with the Levites just as the Lord had commanded Moses concerning them.
text.Rotherham: And Moses and Aaron and all the assembly of the sons of Israel did thus unto the Levites,—according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses as touching the Levites, so, did the sons of Israel unto them.

text.NRSV: The Levites purified themselves from sin and washed their clothes; then Aaron presented them as an elevation offering before the Lord, and Aaron made atonement for them to cleanse them.
text.Rotherham: And the Levites accepted the cleansing from sin and washed their clothes, and Aaron offered them as a wave-offering before Yahweh,—and Aaron put a propitiatory-covering over them to make them pure.

text.NRSV: Thereafter the Levites went in to do their service in the tent of meeting in attendance on Aaron and his sons. As the Lord had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so they did with them.
text.Rotherham: And after that, went the Levites in to do their laborious work in the tent of meeting, before Aaron and before his sons,—as Yahweh commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so, did they unto them.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying;

text.NRSV: This applies to the Levites: from twenty-five years old and upward they shall begin to do duty in the service of the tent of meeting;
text.Rotherham: This is what concerneth the Levites,—from twenty-five years old and upwards, shall they enter to take rank in the host, for doing the laborious work of the tent of meeting;

text.NRSV: and from the age of fifty years they shall retire from the duty of the service and serve no more.
text.Rotherham: but from the age of fifty years, shall they retire from taking rank in the labour, and shall do laborious work no more;

text.NRSV: They may assist their brothers in the tent of meeting in carrying out their duties, but they shall perform no service. Thus you shall do with the Levites in assigning their duties.
text.Rotherham: yet shall they wait upon their brethren in the tent of meeting, by keeping charge, but laborious work, shall they not perform,—thus, shalt thou do unto the Levites, as touching their charges.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after they had come out of the land of Egypt, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses in the desert of Sinai, in the second year by their coming forth out of the land of Egypt in the first month, saying:

text.NRSV: Let the Israelites keep the passover at its appointed time.
text.Rotherham: Let the sons of Israel therefore, keep the passover in its appointed season:

text.NRSV: On the fourteenth day of this month, at twilight, you shall keep it at its appointed time; according to all its statutes and all its regulations you shall keep it.
text.Rotherham: on the fourteenth day of this month between the two evenings, shall ye keep it, in its appointed season,—according to all the statute thereof and according to all the regulations thereof, shall ye keep it.

text.NRSV: So Moses told the Israelites that they should keep the passover.
text.Rotherham: Then spake Moses unto the sons of Israel that they should keep the passover.

text.NRSV: They kept the passover in the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, at twilight, in the wilderness of Sinai. Just as the Lord had commanded Moses, so the Israelites did.
text.Rotherham: So they kept the passover in the first [month], on the fourteenth day of the month between the two evenings, in the desert of Sinai,—according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now there were certain people who were unclean through touching a corpse, so that they could not keep the passover on that day. They came before Moses and Aaron on that day,
text.Rotherham: But so it was that there were, certain men who had become unclean by a dead person, and could not keep the passover on that day,—therefore came they near before Moses and before Aaron on that day.

text.NRSV: and said to him, "Although we are unclean through touching a corpse, why must we be kept from presenting the Lord's offering at its appointed time among the Israelites?"
text.Rotherham: Then said those men unto him, We, are unclean by a dead person,—wherefore should we become of less esteem for want of offering the oblation of Yahweh in its appointed season, in the midst of the sons of Israel?

text.NRSV: Moses spoke to them, "Wait, so that I may hear what the Lord will command concerning you."
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto them,—Stay, and let me hear, what Yahweh shall command concerning you!

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites, saying: Anyone of you or your descendants who is unclean through touching a corpse, or is away on a journey, shall still keep the passover to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying,—Though, any man, be unclean by a dead person or be on a journey afar off, whether in the case of yourselves or of your generations, yet shall he keep a passover unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: In the second month on the fourteenth day, at twilight, they shall keep it; they shall eat it with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.
text.Rotherham: In the second month, on the fourteenth day between the two evenings, shall they keep it,—with unleavened cakes and bitter herbs, shall they eat it.

text.NRSV: They shall leave none of it until morning, nor break a bone of it; according to all the statute for the passover they shall keep it.
text.Rotherham: They shall not leave thereof until morning, and no bone, shall they break therein—according to all the statutes of the passover, shall they keep it.

text.NRSV: But anyone who is clean and is not on a journey, and yet refrains from keeping the passover, shall be cut off from the people for not presenting the Lord's offering at its appointed time; such a one shall bear the consequences for the sin.
text.Rotherham: But as for the man who is, clean, and no a journey, doth not chance to be and yet faileth to keep the passover, that person shall he cut off from among his kinsfolk,—for the oblation of Yahweh, hath he not offered in its appointed season, his own sin, shall, that man, bear.

text.NRSV: Any alien residing among you who wishes to keep the passover to the Lord shall do so according to the statute of the passover and according to its regulation; you shall have one statute for both the resident alien and the native.
text.Rotherham: When moreover there may sojourn with you a sojourner, who would keep a passover unto Yahweh, according to the statute of the passover, and according to the regulation thereof, so, must he keep [it],—one statute, shall there be for you, both for the sojourner and for the native of the land.

text.NRSV: On the day the tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered the tabernacle, the tent of the covenant; and from evening until morning it was over the tabernacle, having the appearance of fire.
text.Rotherham: Now, on the day the habitation was reared, the cloud covered the habitation, even the tent of the testimony,—and in the evening, it was over the habitation, like an appearance of fire, until morning,

text.NRSV: It was always so: the cloud covered it by day, and the appearance of fire by night.
text.Rotherham: Thus, came it to pass continually, the cloud, covered it,—and an appearance of fire, by night;

text.NRSV: Whenever the cloud lifted from over the tent, then the Israelites would set out; and in the place where the cloud settled down, there the Israelites would camp.
text.Rotherham: but at the bidding of the upgoings of the cloud from off the tent, then, after that, the sons of Israel set forward,—and in the place where the cloud abode, there the sons of Israel encamped:

text.NRSV: At the command of the Lord the Israelites would set out, and at the command of the Lord they would camp. As long as the cloud rested over the tabernacle, they would remain in camp.
text.Rotherham: at the bidding of Yahweh, the sons of Israel set forward, and at the bidding of Yahweh, they encamped,—all the days that the cloud abode upon the habitation, they remained encamped.

text.NRSV: Even when the cloud continued over the tabernacle many days, the Israelites would keep the charge of the Lord, and would not set out.
text.Rotherham: And when the cloud lengthened out its stay upon the habitation, many days, then would the sons of Israel keep the watch of Yahweh, and would not set forward.

text.NRSV: Sometimes the cloud would remain a few days over the tabernacle, and according to the command of the Lord they would remain in camp; then according to the command of the Lord they would set out.
text.Rotherham: And, so it was, when the cloud would be a few days upon the habitation, at the bidding of Yahweh, they remained encamped, and at the bidding of Yahweh, they set forward.

text.NRSV: Sometimes the cloud would remain from evening until morning; and when the cloud lifted in the morning, they would set out, or if it continued for a day and a night, when the cloud lifted they would set out.
text.Rotherham: And, so it was, when the cloud would be from evening until morning, as soon as the cloud went up in the morning, so soon did they set forward: whether by day or by night, as soon as the cloud went up, so soon did they set forward.

text.NRSV: Whether it was two days, or a month, or a longer time, that the cloud continued over the tabernacle, remaining above it, the Israelites would remain in camp and would not set out; but when it lifted they would set out.
text.Rotherham: Whether for two days or a month or a year, the cloud lengthened out its stay upon the habitation abiding thereupon, the sons of Israel remained encamped and set not forward,—but at the upgoings thereof, they set forward.

text.NRSV: At the command of the Lord they would camp, and at the command of the Lord they would set out. They kept the charge of the Lord, at the command of the Lord by Moses.
text.Rotherham: At the bidding of Yahweh, they encamped, and at the bidding of Yahweh, they set forward,—the watch of Yahweh, they kept, at the bidding of Yahweh by the hand of Moses.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: Make two silver trumpets; you shall make them of hammered work; and you shall use them for summoning the congregation, and for breaking camp.
text.Rotherham: Make thee two trumpets of silver, of beaten work, shalt thou make them,—and they shall be unto thee for calling the assembly, and for setting forward the camps.

text.NRSV: When both are blown, the whole congregation shall assemble before you at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: As soon as they blow therewith, so soon shall all the assembly assemble themselves unto thee, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: But if only one is blown, then the leaders, the heads of the tribes of Israel, shall assemble before you.
text.Rotherham: And, if, only once, they blow, then shall the princes, the heads of the thousands of Israel, gather themselves unto thee.

text.NRSV: When you blow an alarm, the camps on the east side shall set out;
text.Rotherham: But when ye blow an alarm, then shall set forward the camps that are encamped eastwards;

text.NRSV: when you blow a second alarm, the camps on the south side shall set out. An alarm is to be blown whenever they are to set out.
text.Rotherham: and when ye blow a second alarm, then shall set forward the camps that are encamped southwards,—an alarm, shall they blow for setting them forward,

text.NRSV: But when the assembly is to be gathered, you shall blow, but you shall not sound an alarm.
text.Rotherham: But in calling together the convocation, ye shall blow but shall not sound an alarm.

text.NRSV: The sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow the trumpets; this shall be a perpetual institution throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Aaron the priests, shall blow with the trumpets,—and it shall be unto you for a statute age-abiding, unto your generations.

text.NRSV: When you go to war in your land against the adversary who oppresses you, you shall sound an alarm with the trumpets, so that you may be remembered before the Lord your God, and be saved from your enemies.
text.Rotherham: And when ye go into war in your land against the assailant that assaileth you, then shall ye blow an alarm with the trumpets,—and bring yourselves to mind before Yahweh your God, and be saved, from your enemies,

text.NRSV: Also on your days of rejoicing, at your appointed festivals, and at the beginnings of your months, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and over your sacrifices of well-being, and they shall serve as a reminder on your behalf before the Lord your God: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And in your day of rejoicing and in your appointed seasons and in the beginnings of your months, then shall ye blow with the trumpets, over your ascending-sacrifices, and over your peace-offerings,—so shall they be unto you for a memorial before your God. I—Yahweh, am your God.

text.NRSV: In the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth day of the month, the cloud lifted from over the tabernacle of the covenant.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass in the second year, in the second month on the twentieth of the month, that the cloud lifted itself up, from off the habitation of the testimony;

text.NRSV: Then the Israelites set out by stages from the wilderness of Sinai; and the cloud settled down in the wilderness of Paran.
text.Rotherham: and the sons of Israel set forward by their removals from the desert of Sinai, and then the cloud abode in the desert of Paran.

text.NRSV: They set out for the first time at the command of the Lord by Moses.
text.Rotherham: Thus then did they set forward for the first time,—at the bidding of Yahweh, by the hand of Moses.

text.NRSV: The standard of the camp of Judah set out first, company by company, and over the whole company was Nahshon son of Amminadab.
text.Rotherham: So the standard of the camp of the sons of Judah set forward first, by their hosts, and over his own host, was Nahshon, son of Amminadab;

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Issachar was Nethanel son of Zuar;
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Issachar, Nethanel, son of Zuar;

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Zebulun was Eliab son of Helon.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Zebulun, Eliab, son of Helon.

text.NRSV: Then the tabernacle was taken down, and the Gershonites and the Merarites, who carried the tabernacle, set out.
text.Rotherham: Then was taken down the habitation,—and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set forward bearing the habitation.

text.NRSV: Next the standard of the camp of Reuben set out, company by company; and over the whole company was Elizur son of Shedeur.
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the standard of the camp of Reuben, by their hosts,—and over his own host, Elizur, son of Shedeur;

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Simeon was Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai,
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Simeon, Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai;

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Gad was Eliasaph son of Deuel.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Gad, Eliasaph, son of Deuel.

text.NRSV: Then the Kohathites set out, carrying the holy things. (The tabernacle was set up before their arrival.)
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the Kohathites, bearing the sanctuary,—and so the habitation was reared by the time they came in.

text.NRSV: Next the standard of the Ephraimite camp set out, company by company, and over the whole company was Elishama son of Ammihud.
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the standard of the camp of the sons of Ephraim by their hosts,—and over his own host, was Elishama son of Ammihud;

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Manasseh was Gamaliel son of Pedahzur,
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Manasseh, Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur;

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Benjamin was Abidan son of Gideoni.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, Abidan, son of Gideoni.

text.NRSV: Then the standard of the camp of Dan, acting as the rear guard of all the camps, set out, company by company: Over the whole company was Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the standard of the camp of the sons of Dan, bringing up the rear of all the camps, by their hosts,—and over his own host, Ahiezur, son of Ammishaddai;

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Asher was Pagiel son of Ochran,
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Asher, Pagiel, son of Ochran;

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Naphtali was Ahira son of Enan.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali, Ahira, son of Enan.

text.NRSV: This was the order of march of the Israelites, company by company, when they set out.
text.Rotherham: These, were the settings-forward of the sons of Israel by their hosts,—thus did they set forward.

text.NRSV: Moses said to Hobab son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law, "We are setting out for the place of which the Lord said, 'I will give it to you'; come with us, and we will treat you well; for the Lord has promised good to Israel."
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses to Hobab, son of Raguel the Midianite, father-in-law of Moses: Setting forward, are we unto the place of which Yahweh hath said, The same, will I give unto you,—Oh come with us and we will do thee good, for, Yahweh, hath spoken good, concerning Israel.

text.NRSV: But he said to him, "I will not go, but I will go back to my own land and to my kindred."
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him, I will not go,—but unto my own land, and unto my own kindred, will I go.

text.NRSV: He said, "Do not leave us, for you know where we should camp in the wilderness, and you will serve as eyes for us.
text.Rotherham: Then said he: I beseech thee, do not forsake us,—for, on this account, hast thou come to know of our encamping in the desert, so shalt thou be unto us as eyes;

text.NRSV: Moreover, if you go with us, whatever good the Lord does for us, the same we will do for you."
text.Rotherham: and it shall be if thou wilt go with us,—yea it shall be that with the very good wherewith Yahweh shall do us good, will we do good unto thee.

text.NRSV: So they set out from the mount of the Lord three days' journey with the ark of the covenant of the Lord going before them three days' journey, to seek out a resting place for them,
text.Rotherham: So then they went forward, from the mountain of Yahweh, a journey of three days,—and the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, was going before them a journey of three days, to search out for them a resting-place,

text.NRSV: the cloud of the Lord being over them by day when they set out from the camp.
text.Rotherham: And the cloud of Yahweh, was over them by day,—when they set forward out of the camp.

text.NRSV: Whenever the ark set out, Moses would say, "Arise, O Lord, let your enemies be scattered, and your foes flee before you."
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when the ark set forward, that Moses said: Arise, O Yahweh! and scattered be thy foes, Let them that hate thee flee before thee;

text.NRSV: And whenever it came to rest, he would say, "Return, O Lord of the ten thousand thousands of Israel."
text.Rotherham: But when it rested, he said,—Return, O Yahweh! unto the myriads of the thousands of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now when the people complained in the hearing of the Lord about their misfortunes, the Lord heard it and his anger was kindled. Then the fire of the Lord burned against them, and consumed some outlying parts of the camp.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that when the people were giving themselves up to murmuring, it was grievous in the ears of Yahweh,—so Yahweh hearkened, and kindled was his anger, and the fire of Yahweh burned among them, and consumed the uttermost part of the camp.

text.NRSV: But the people cried out to Moses; and Moses prayed to the Lord, and the fire abated.
text.Rotherham: Then did the people make outcry unto Moses,—and Moses prayed unto Yahweh, and the fire sank down.

text.NRSV: So that place was called Taberah, because the fire of the Lord burned against them.
text.Rotherham: So he called the name of that place, Taberah [="A Burning"] because there burned among them the fire of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The rabble among them had a strong craving; and the Israelites also wept again, and said, "If only we had meat to eat!
text.Rotherham: Moreover the mixed multitude that was in their midst concealed not their lusting,—and so even the sons of Israel, fell away and wept, and said: Who will grant us to eat flesh?

text.NRSV: We remember the fish we used to eat in Egypt for nothing, the cucumbers, the melons, the leeks, the onions, and the garlic;
text.Rotherham: We remember the fish that we did eat in Egypt, without money,—the cucumbers, and the water-melons, and the leeks and the onions, and the garlick.

text.NRSV: but now our strength is dried up, and there is nothing at all but this manna to look at."
text.Rotherham: But, now, our soul is dried up, there is nothing at all,—unless, unto the manna, [we turn] our eyes.

text.NRSV: Now the manna was like coriander seed, and its color was like the color of gum resin.
text.Rotherham: Now, the manna, was like coriander seed, and the appearance thereof like the appearance of bdellium,

text.NRSV: The people went around and gathered it, ground it in mills or beat it in mortars, then boiled it in pots and made cakes of it; and the taste of it was like the taste of cakes baked with oil.
text.Rotherham: The people used to go about and pick it up, and grind it with a pair of mill-stones, or pound it in a mortar, and boil it in a pot, and make it into round cakes,—then was the taste thereof like the taste of a sweet cake made with oil.

text.NRSV: When the dew fell on the camp in the night, the manna would fall with it.
text.Rotherham: When the dew came down upon the camp by night, the manna came down thereupon.

text.NRSV: Moses heard the people weeping throughout their families, all at the entrances of their tents. Then the Lord became very angry, and Moses was displeased.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses heard the people weeping by their families, every one at the entrance of his tent,—then kindled the anger of Yahweh fiercely, and in the eyes of Moses, it was grievous.

text.NRSV: So Moses said to the Lord, "Why have you treated your servant so badly? Why have I not found favor in your sight, that you lay the burden of all this people on me?
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Yahweh—Wherefore hast thou let thy servant come to grief, and wherefore have I not found favour in thine eyes,—that thou shouldest lay the burden of all this people upon me?

text.NRSV: Did I conceive all this people? Did I give birth to them, that you should say to me, 'Carry them in your bosom, as a nurse carries a sucking child,' to the land that you promised on oath to their ancestors?
text.Rotherham: Did, I, conceive all this people, or, I, beget them,—that thou shouldest say unto me, Carry them in thy bosom, as a nursing father carrieth a suckling, unto the soil which thou didst swear unto their fathers?

text.NRSV: Where am I to get meat to give to all this people? For they come weeping to me and say, 'Give us meat to eat!'
text.Rotherham: Whence should, I, have flesh to give to all this people,—for they keep weeping by me saying, Oh give us flesh that we may eat!

text.NRSV: I am not able to carry all this people alone, for they are too heavy for me.
text.Rotherham: Unable, am, I, by myself, to carry all this people,—for they are too heavy for me.

text.NRSV: If this is the way you are going to treat me, put me to death at once—if I have found favor in your sight—and do not let me see my misery."
text.Rotherham: But if, in this way, thou art going to deal with me, slay me, I beseech thee, slay, if I have found favour in thine eyes,—and let me not see my grief,

text.NRSV: So the Lord said to Moses, "Gather for me seventy of the elders of Israel, whom you know to be the elders of the people and officers over them; bring them to the tent of meeting, and have them take their place there with you.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Gather thou unto me seventy men, from among the elders of Israel, of whom thou knowest that they are elders of the people and their overseers,—then shalt thou take them unto the tent of meeting, and they shall station themselves there with thee.

text.NRSV: I will come down and talk with you there; and I will take some of the spirit that is on you and put it on them; and they shall bear the burden of the people along with you so that you will not bear it all by yourself.
text.Rotherham: Then will I come down, and speak with thee there, and will take of the spirit that is upon thee and put upon them,—and they shall carry, with thee, the burden of the people, and, thou, shalt not carry it by thyself.

text.NRSV: And say to the people: Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, and you shall eat meat; for you have wailed in the hearing of the Lord, saying, 'If only we had meat to eat! Surely it was better for us in Egypt.' Therefore the Lord will give you meat, and you shall eat.
text.Rotherham: And, unto the people, shalt thou say: Hallow yourselves by to-morrow, and ye shall eat flesh, for ye have wept in the ears of Yahweh saying—Who will grant us to eat flesh? for it was well with us in Egypt,—so then Yahweh will give you flesh and ye shall eat.

text.NRSV: You shall eat not only one day, or two days, or five days, or ten days, or twenty days,
text.Rotherham: Not one day, shall ye eat nor two days,—nor five days nor ten days, nor twenty days:

text.NRSV: but for a whole month—until it comes out of your nostrils and becomes loathsome to you—because you have rejected the Lord who is among you, and have wailed before him, saying, 'Why did we ever leave Egypt?'"
text.Rotherham: for a month of days until it cometh forth out of your nostrils, so shall it become to you a loathsome thing,—because ye have refused Yahweh who is in your midst, and have wept before him, saying, Wherefore now came we forth out of Egypt?

text.NRSV: But Moses said, "The people I am with number six hundred thousand on foot; and you say, 'I will give them meat, that they may eat for a whole month'!
text.Rotherham: And Moses said, Six hundred thousand footmen, are the people in whose midst am, I, yet thou hast said—Flesh, will I give unto them, and they shall eat for a month of days.

text.NRSV: Are there enough flocks and herds to slaughter for them? Are there enough fish in the sea to catch for them?"
text.Rotherham: Shall the flocks and herds, be slaughtered for them, that it may suffice for them? Shall all the fishes of the sea, be gathered together unto them that it may suffice for them?

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, "Is the Lord's power limited? Now you shall see whether my word will come true for you or not."
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Shall the hand of Yahweh, fail? Now, shalt thou see whether my word come to pass unto thee or not.

text.NRSV: So Moses went out and told the people the words of the Lord; and he gathered seventy elders of the people, and placed them all around the tent.
text.Rotherham: And Moses went forth and spake unto the people the words of Yahweh,—and gathered together seventy men from among the elders of the people, and caused them to stand round about the tent.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord came down in the cloud and spoke to him, and took some of the spirit that was on him and put it on the seventy elders; and when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied. But they did not do so again.
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh came down, in the cloud, and spake unto him, and took of the spirit that was upon him, and gave unto the seventy men—the elders. And it came to pass that when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied, and then did so no more.

text.NRSV: Two men remained in the camp, one named Eldad, and the other named Medad, and the spirit rested on them; they were among those registered, but they had not gone out to the tent, and so they prophesied in the camp.
text.Rotherham: Now there were two men left behind in the camp—the name of the one, was Eldad and the name of the other, Medad, so then the spirit, rested on them—they, being among them who were written, though they had not gone forth unto the tent,—but they prophesied in the camp.

text.NRSV: And a young man ran and told Moses, "Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp."
text.Rotherham: And there ran a young man, and told Moses and said,—Eldad and Medad, are prophesying in the camp!

text.NRSV: And Joshua son of Nun, the assistant of Moses, one of his chosen men, said, "My lord Moses, stop them!"
text.Rotherham: Then responded Joshua son of Nun the attendant of Moses from his youth, and said,—My lord Moses forbid them!

text.NRSV: But Moses said to him, "Are you jealous for my sake? Would that all the Lord's people were prophets, and that the Lord would put his spirit on them!"
text.Rotherham: But Moses said unto him, Art, thou, jealous for, me? Oh would that, all the people of Yahweh, were prophets! Yea let Yahweh put his spirit upon them!

text.NRSV: And Moses and the elders of Israel returned to the camp.
text.Rotherham: Then was Moses received back into the camp,—he and the elders of Israel.

text.NRSV: Then a wind went out from the Lord, and it brought quails from the sea and let them fall beside the camp, about a day's journey on this side and a day's journey on the other side, all around the camp, about two cubits deep on the ground.
text.Rotherham: Now, a wind, had sprung up, from Yahweh, and cut off quails from the sea, and let them lie over the camp—as it were a days journey here and a days journey there, round about the camp,—and as it were two cubits on the face of the land.

text.NRSV: So the people worked all that day and night and all the next day, gathering the quails; the least anyone gathered was ten homers; and they spread them out for themselves all around the camp.
text.Rotherham: And the people rose up all that day and all the night and all the next day, and gathered the quails, he that did least, gathered ten homers,—and they spread them out for themselves spreading away, round about the camp.

text.NRSV: But while the meat was still between their teeth, before it was consumed, the anger of the Lord was kindled against the people, and the Lord struck the people with a very great plague.
text.Rotherham: The flesh, was yet between their teeth, not yet chewed,—when the anger of Yahweh, had kindled upon the people, and Yahweh smote the people, with an exceeding great plague.

text.NRSV: So that place was called Kibroth-hattaavah, because there they buried the people who had the craving.
text.Rotherham: And the name of that place was called Kibroth-hattaavah—because there, they buried the people who had lusted,

text.NRSV: From Kibroth-hattaavah the people journeyed to Hazeroth.
text.Rotherham: From Kibroth-hattaavah, the people set forward to Hazeroth,—and they remained in Hazeroth.

text.NRSV: While they were at Hazeroth, Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses because of the Cushite woman whom he had married (for he had indeed married a Cushite woman);
text.Rotherham: And Miriam and Aaron spake against Moses, on account of the Cushite woman whom he had taken,—for, a Cushite woman, had he taken.

text.NRSV: and they said, "Has the Lord spoken only through Moses? Has he not spoken through us also?" And the Lord heard it.
text.Rotherham: And they said: Is it only and solely with Moses, that Yahweh hath spoken? With us also, hath he not spoken? And Yahweh heard it.

text.NRSV: Now the man Moses was very humble, more so than anyone else on the face of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Now, the man Moses, was patient, exceedingly,—above all the sons of earth, who were on the face of the ground.

text.NRSV: Suddenly the Lord said to Moses, Aaron, and Miriam, "Come out, you three, to the tent of meeting." So the three of them came out.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh suddenly, unto Moses, and unto Aaron, and unto Miriam, Come out ye three unto the tent of meeting. And they three went out.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord came down in a pillar of cloud, and stood at the entrance of the tent, and called Aaron and Miriam; and they both came forward.
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh came down in a pillar of cloud, and stood at the entrance of the tent,—and called Aaron and Miriam, and they both went forth.

text.NRSV: And he said, "Hear my words: When there are prophets among you, I the Lord make myself known to them in visions; I speak to them in dreams.
text.Rotherham: Then said he Hear, I beseech you my words. When ye have your prophet, As Yahweh in a vision, will I make myself known, unto him; In a dream, will I speak with him.

text.NRSV: Not so with my servant Moses; he is entrusted with all my house.
text.Rotherham: Not so, my servant Moses,—In all my house, trusty, is he:

text.NRSV: With him I speak face to face—clearly, not in riddles; and he beholds the form of the Lord. Why then were you not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?"
text.Rotherham: Mouth to mouth, do I speak with him And plainly—not in dark sayings, And the form of Yahweh, doth he discern,—Wherefore then, were ye not afraid to speak against my servant—against Moses?

text.NRSV: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against them, and he departed.
text.Rotherham: And the anger of Yahweh kindled upon them and he departed.

text.NRSV: When the cloud went away from over the tent, Miriam had become leprous, as white as snow. And Aaron turned towards Miriam and saw that she was leprous.
text.Rotherham: And, when, the cloud, removed from off the tent,—lo! Miriam, leprous, like snow! And Aaron turned unto Miriam and lo! …leprous!

text.NRSV: Then Aaron said to Moses, "Oh, my lord, do not punish us for a sin that we have so foolishly committed.
text.Rotherham: Then said Aaron unto Moses,—Oh my lord, do not I beseech thee lay upon us sin, although we have made ourselves foolish, and although we have sinned.

text.NRSV: Do not let her be like one stillborn, whose flesh is half consumed when it comes out of its mother's womb."
text.Rotherham: Let her not, I beseech thee, remain like the still-born,—which when it is born of its mother, the half of its flesh is consumed.

text.NRSV: And Moses cried to the Lord, "O God, please heal her."
text.Rotherham: Then Moses made outcry unto Yahweh, saying,—O GOD, I beseech thee grant healing. I beseech thee unto her.

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to Moses, "If her father had but spit in her face, would she not bear her shame for seven days? Let her be shut out of the camp for seven days, and after that she may be brought in again."
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses: If, her own father, had, but spat, in her face, would she not, have acknowledged the shame for seven days? Let her shut herself up for seven days, outside the camp, and afterwards, let her be received back.

text.NRSV: So Miriam was shut out of the camp for seven days; and the people did not set out on the march until Miriam had been brought in again.
text.Rotherham: So Miriam shut herself up, outside the camp, for seven days,—and, the people, set not forward, until Miriam had been received back.

text.NRSV: After that the people set out from Hazeroth, and camped in the wilderness of Paran.
text.Rotherham: Then afterwards, did the people set forward from Hazeroth, and encamped in the desert of Paran.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:

text.NRSV: "Send men to spy out the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the Israelites; from each of their ancestral tribes you shall send a man, every one a leader among them."
text.Rotherham: Send for thee men and let them spy out the land of Canaan, which I am giving unto the sons of Israel,—one man each for the tribe of his fathers, shalt thou send, each one a prince among them.

text.NRSV: So Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran, according to the command of the Lord, all of them leading men among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses sent them out of the desert of Paran, at the bidding of Yahweh,—all of them, great men, heads of the sons of Israel, were they.

text.NRSV: These were their names: From the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur;
text.Rotherham: And, these, are their names,—Of the tribe of Reuben, Shammua, son of Zaccur;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat, son of Hori;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb, son of Jephunneh;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Issachar, Igal, son of Joseph;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Nun;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea, son of Nun;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Palti, son of Raphu;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel, son of Sodi;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Joseph (that is, from the tribe of Manasseh), Gaddi son of Susi;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Joseph—of the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi son of Susi;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel, son of Gemalli;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Asher, Sethur, son of Michael;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi, son of Vophsi;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Gad, Geuel, son of Machi.

text.NRSV: These were the names of the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses changed the name of Hoshea son of Nun to Joshua.
text.Rotherham: These, are the names of the men, whom Moses sent to spy out the land,—but Moses called Hoshea son of Nun, Joshua.

text.NRSV: Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan, and said to them, "Go up there into the Negeb, and go up into the hill country,
text.Rotherham: So Moses sent them, to spy out the land of Canaan,—and said unto them—Go up, here in the South, Yea ye shall go up the mountain;

text.NRSV: and see what the land is like, and whether the people who live in it are strong or weak, whether they are few or many,
text.Rotherham: And shall see the land, what it is,—And the people that dwell thereupon, Whether they are, strong, or weak, Whether they are, few or many;

text.NRSV: and whether the land they live in is good or bad, and whether the towns that they live in are unwalled or fortified,
text.Rotherham: And what, the land, is, wherein they dwell, Whether it is, good, or, bad,—And the cities within which they dwell, Whether in camps or in strongholds;

text.NRSV: and whether the land is rich or poor, and whether there are trees in it or not. Be bold, and bring some of the fruit of the land." Now it was the season of the first ripe grapes.
text.Rotherham: And what the land is, Whether, fat, or, lean, Whether there is timber therein or is not, Then shall ye pluck up courage and take of the fruit of the land. Now the days, were the days of the first-fruits of grapes.

text.NRSV: So they went up and spied out the land from the wilderness of Zin to Rehob, near Lebo-hamath.
text.Rotherham: So they went up, and spied out the land,—from the wilderness of Zin unto Rehob at the entering in of Hamath.

text.NRSV: They went up into the Negeb, and came to Hebron; and Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the Anakites, were there. (Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.)
text.Rotherham: Yea they went up in the South and entered as far as Hebron, and there, were Ahiman Sheshai and Talmai descendants of the Anak. Now, Hebron, was built seven years, before Zoan of Egypt.

text.NRSV: And they came to the Wadi Eshcol, and cut down from there a branch with a single cluster of grapes, and they carried it on a pole between two of them. They also brought some pomegranates and figs.
text.Rotherham: And they entered as far as the ravine of Eschol, and cut down, from thence a branch with one cluster of grapes, and bare it on a pole between two,—also of the pomegranates and of the figs.

text.NRSV: That place was called the Wadi Eshcol, because of the cluster that the Israelites cut down from there.
text.Rotherham: That place, was called, The Ravine of Eschol [="Cluster-ravine"]—on account of the cluster, which was cut down from thence by the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: At the end of forty days they returned from spying out the land.
text.Rotherham: And they returned from spying out the land,—at the end of forty days.

text.NRSV: And they came to Moses and Aaron and to all the congregation of the Israelites in the wilderness of Paran, at Kadesh; they brought back word to them and to all the congregation, and showed them the fruit of the land.
text.Rotherham: So they took their journey, and came in unto Moses and unto Aaron and unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel into the wilderness of Paran, towards Kadesh,—and brought back word—unto them and unto all the assembly, and showed them the fruit of the land.

text.NRSV: And they told him, "We came to the land to which you sent us; it flows with milk and honey, and this is its fruit.
text.Rotherham: And they recounted unto him, and said, We entered into the land whither thou didst send us, and surely it doth flow with milk and honey, and, this, is the fruit thereof.

text.NRSV: Yet the people who live in the land are strong, and the towns are fortified and very large; and besides, we saw the descendants of Anak there.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless surely mighty, are the people that dwell in the land,—and, the cities, walled in, exceeding great, moreover also, the descendants of the Anak, saw we there.

text.NRSV: The Amalekites live in the land of the Negeb; the Hittites, the Jebusites, and the Amorites live in the hill country; and the Canaanites live by the sea, and along the Jordan."
text.Rotherham: Amalek, dwelleth in the land of the South; And, the Hittite, and the Jebusite and the Amorite, dwell in the mountain And the Canaanite, dwelleth by the sea, and by the side of the Jordan.

text.NRSV: But Caleb quieted the people before Moses, and said, "Let us go up at once and occupy it, for we are well able to overcome it."
text.Rotherham: And Caleb stilled the people unto Moses,—and said unto them—Let us go straight up, and possess it, for we shall, surely prevail, against it.

text.NRSV: Then the men who had gone up with him said, "We are not able to go up against this people, for they are stronger than we."
text.Rotherham: But the men who went up with him said, We cannot go up against the people,—for stronger, are they than we.

text.NRSV: So they brought to the Israelites an unfavorable report of the land that they had spied out, saying, "The land that we have gone through as spies is a land that devours its inhabitants; and all the people that we saw in it are of great size.
text.Rotherham: So they sent forth a rumour of the land which they had spied out, among the sons of Israel, saying,—As touching the land through which we passed to spy it out, it is, land that eateth up them who dwell therein, And all the people that we saw in the midst thereof, were men of great stature.

text.NRSV: There we saw the Nephilim (the Anakites come from the Nephilim); and to ourselves we seemed like grasshoppers, and so we seemed to them."
text.Rotherham: And, there, saw we the giants, sons of Anak [descended] of the giants,—And we were in our own eyes, as grass-hoppers, And, so, were we in their eyes.

text.NRSV: Then all the congregation raised a loud cry, and the people wept that night.
text.Rotherham: Then did all the assembly lift up, and give forth their voice,—and the people wept throughout that night.

text.NRSV: And all the Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron; the whole congregation said to them, "Would that we had died in the land of Egypt! Or would that we had died in this wilderness!
text.Rotherham: And all the sons of Israel, murmured against Moses and against Aaron,—and all the assembly said unto them—Oh that we had died in the land of Egypt, Or in this wilderness, oh that we had died!

text.NRSV: Why is the Lord bringing us into this land to fall by the sword? Our wives and our little ones will become booty; would it not be better for us to go back to Egypt?"
text.Rotherham: Wherefore then is Yahweh bringing us into this land, to fall by the sword? Our wives and little ones, will become a prey! Would it not be well for us to go back towards Egypt?

text.NRSV: So they said to one another, "Let us choose a captain, and go back to Egypt."
text.Rotherham: And they said, one to another,—Let us appoint a head and let us go back towards Egypt.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before all the assembly of the congregation of the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Then did Moses and Aaron fall upon their faces,—before all the convocation of the assembly of the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh, who were among those who had spied out the land, tore their clothes
text.Rotherham: But, Joshua son of Nun, and Caleb son of Jephunneh, of them who spied out the land, rent their clothes;

text.NRSV: and said to all the congregation of the Israelites, "The land that we went through as spies is an exceedingly good land.
text.Rotherham: and spake unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel, saying,—As for the land through which we passed to spy it out, Good, is the land, exceedingly,

text.NRSV: If the Lord is pleased with us, he will bring us into this land and give it to us, a land that flows with milk and honey.
text.Rotherham: If Yahweh delight in us, then will he bring us into this land, and give it unto us,—A land which doth flow with milk and honey.

text.NRSV: Only, do not rebel against the Lord; and do not fear the people of the land, for they are no more than bread for us; their protection is removed from them, and the Lord is with us; do not fear them."
text.Rotherham: Only against Yahweh, do not rebel. Do not, ye, fear the people of the land, For our food, they are,—Their shade hath departed, from over them. And Yahweh is with us, Do not fear them!

text.NRSV: But the whole congregation threatened to stone them. Then the glory of the Lord appeared at the tent of meeting to all the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: But when all the assembly bade stone them with stones, the glory of Yahweh, appeared, in the tent of meeting, unto all the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to Moses, "How long will this people despise me? And how long will they refuse to believe in me, in spite of all the signs that I have done among them?"
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses How long! must this people, despise me? And how long can they not believe in me. in view of all the signs which I have done in their midst?

text.NRSV: I will strike them with pestilence and disinherit them, and I will make of you a nation greater and mightier than they."
text.Rotherham: Let me smite them with pestilence, and disinherit them,—And make thee into a nation greater and more mighty than they!

text.NRSV: But Moses said to the Lord, "Then the Egyptians will hear of it, for in your might you brought up this people from among them,"
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto Yahweh,—So shall the Egyptians hear, For thou hast brought up. in thy might, this people out of their midst;

text.NRSV: and they will tell the inhabitants of this land. They have heard that you, O Lord, are in the midst of this people; for you, O Lord, are seen face to face, and your cloud stands over them and you go in front of them, in a pillar of cloud by day and in a pillar of fire by night.
text.Rotherham: And will tell it unto the inhabitants of this land: They have heard, That, thou, Yahweh, art in the midst of this people,—That eye to eye, is he seen—thou, Yahweh, And thy cloud, is standing over them, And in a pillar of cloud, thou thyself, art going on before them by day, And in a pillar of fire, by night.

text.NRSV: Now if you kill this people all at one time, then the nations who have heard about you will say,
text.Rotherham: As soon, therefore, as thou hast put to death this people as one man, so soon will the nations who have heard thy fame speak saying:

text.NRSV: 'It is because the Lord was not able to bring this people into the land he swore to give them that he has slaughtered them in the wilderness.'
text.Rotherham: Because Yahweh, was not able, to bring in this people into the land which he had sworn unto them, therefore did he slay them in the desert.

text.NRSV: And now, therefore, let the power of the Lord be great in the way you promised when you spoke, saying,
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I beseech thee let the might of My Lord, be magnified,—according as thou didst speak saying:

text.NRSV: 'The Lord is slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love, forgiving iniquity and transgression, but by no means clearing the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the parents upon the children to the third and the fourth generation.'
text.Rotherham: Yahweh—slow to anger and great in lovingkindness, Forgiving iniquity and transgression: Though he will not leave, wholly unpunished, Visiting the iniquity of fathers upon sons, Upon a third generation, and upon a fourth.

text.NRSV: Forgive the iniquity of this people according to the greatness of your steadfast love, just as you have pardoned this people, from Egypt even until now."
text.Rotherham: Pardon I beseech thee, the iniquity of this people, According to the greatness of thy lovingkindness,—And according as thou hast forgiven this people, from Egypt even thus far.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, "I do forgive, just as you have asked;
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said, I have pardoned according to thy word;

text.NRSV: nevertheless—as I live, and as all the earth shall be filled with the glory of the Lord—
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless as I live,—and that all the earth may be filled with the glory of Yahweh,

text.NRSV: none of the people who have seen my glory and the signs that I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and yet have tested me these ten times and have not obeyed my voice,
text.Rotherham: Verily, none of the men who have been beholding my glory and my signs, which I have done in Egypt and in the desert,—and have put me to the proof these ten times, and have not hearkened unto my voice,

text.NRSV: shall see the land that I swore to give to their ancestors; none of those who despised me shall see it.
text.Rotherham: shall see the land, which I sware to their fathers,—yea, none of my despisers, shall see it.

text.NRSV: But my servant Caleb, because he has a different spirit and has followed me wholeheartedly, I will bring into the land into which he went, and his descendants shall possess it.
text.Rotherham: But my servant Caleb—because there was another spirit with him, and he followed me fully, therefore will I bring him into the land whereinto he entered, and his seed, shall possess it.

text.NRSV: Now, since the Amalekites and the Canaanites live in the valleys, turn tomorrow and set out for the wilderness by the way to the Red Sea."
text.Rotherham: Now the Amalekites and the Canaanites, are dwelling in the valley,—to-morrow, turn ye away; and set yourselves forward towards the desert by the way of the Red Sea.

text.NRSV: And the Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying:

text.NRSV: How long shall this wicked congregation complain against me? I have heard the complaints of the Israelites, which they complain against me.
text.Rotherham: How long, as regardeth this evil assembly, are they to be murmuring against me? The murmuring of the sons of Israel which, they, have been murmuring against me, have I heard,

text.NRSV: Say to them, "As I live," says the Lord, "I will do to you the very things I heard you say:
text.Rotherham: Say unto them: As I live, is the oracle of Yahweh: Surely as ye have spoken in mine ears, so, will I do unto you:—

text.NRSV: your dead bodies shall fall in this very wilderness; and of all your number, included in the census, from twenty years old and upward, who have complained against me,
text.Rotherham: In this desert, shall your dead bodies fall And none of you who were numbered in all your counting, from twenty years old and upwards,—who have murmured against me,

text.NRSV: not one of you shall come into the land in which I swore to settle you, except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.
text.Rotherham: none of you, shall enter into the land, as to which I uplifted my hand to give you an abode therein,—save Caleb son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun:

text.NRSV: But your little ones, who you said would become booty, I will bring in, and they shall know the land that you have despised.
text.Rotherham: whereas, your little ones, of whom ye said, they should become, a prey, I will bring them in, and they shall acknowledge the land which ye refused.

text.NRSV: But as for you, your dead bodies shall fall in this wilderness.
text.Rotherham: But, your own dead bodies, shall fall in this desert;

text.NRSV: And your children shall be shepherds in the wilderness for forty years, and shall suffer for your faithlessness, until the last of your dead bodies lies in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: and your sons, shall be shepherds in the desert forty years, and shall bear your unchastities,—until your dead bodies are consumed in the desert.

text.NRSV: In accordance with the number of the days in which you spied out the land, forty days, for every day a year, you shall bear your iniquity, forty years, and you shall know my displeasure."
text.Rotherham: By the number of the days wherein ye spied out the land—forty days, each day for a year, shall they bear your iniquities—forty years,—so shall ye know my opposition.

text.NRSV: I the Lord have spoken; surely I will do thus to all this wicked congregation gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall come to a full end, and there they shall die.
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, have spoken, Verily this, will I do to all this evil assembly, who have conspired against me,—In this desert, shall they be consumed And there, shall they die.

text.NRSV: And the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land, who returned and made all the congregation complain against him by bringing a bad report about the land—
text.Rotherham: Now as for the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land,—who returned and caused all the assembly to murmur against him by giving out a rumour against the land,

text.NRSV: the men who brought an unfavorable report about the land died by a plague before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: they died—the men who gave out an evil rumour of the land,—by the plague, before Yahweh.

text.NRSV: But Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh alone remained alive, of those men who went to spy out the land.
text.Rotherham: But, Joshua son of Nun, and Caleb son of Jephunneh! remained alive—of those men who went to spy out the land.

text.NRSV: When Moses told these words to all the Israelites, the people mourned greatly.
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake these words unto all the sons of Israel, and the people were greatly depressed.

text.NRSV: They rose early in the morning and went up to the heights of the hill country, saying, "Here we are. We will go up to the place that the Lord has promised, for we have sinned."
text.Rotherham: So they arose early in the morning, and went up into the top of the mountain, saying,—We are here—therefore will we go up into the place as to which Yahweh hath said that we have sinned.

text.NRSV: But Moses said, "Why do you disobey the command of the Lord? It will not succeed.
text.Rotherham: But Moses said, Wherefore is it that, ye, are going beyond the bidding of Yahweh,—since that cannot prosper?

text.NRSV: Do not go up, for the Lord is not with you; do not let yourselves be struck down before your enemies.
text.Rotherham: Do not go up, For Yahweh is not in your midst,—Lest ye be smitten down before your enemies.

text.NRSV: For the Amalekites and the Canaanites will face you there, and you shall fall by the sword; because you have turned back from following the Lord, the Lord will not be with you."
text.Rotherham: For, the Amalekites and the Canaanites, are there before you, So shall ye fall by the sword,—For inasmuch as ye have turned back from following Yahweh, Therefore will Yahweh not be with you.

text.NRSV: But they presumed to go up to the heights of the hill country, even though the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and Moses, had not left the camp.
text.Rotherham: But they presumptuously ascended into the top of the mountain,—though, neither, the ark of the covenant of Yahweh nor Moses moved out of the midst of the camp.

text.NRSV: Then the Amalekites and the Canaanites who lived in that hill country came down and defeated them, pursuing them as far as Hormah.
text.Rotherham: So ten the Amalekites and the Canaanites, that dwelt in that mountain, came down,—and smote them and routed them even unto Hormah.
